github.com/assafelovic/gpt-researcher.name: assafelovic/gpt-researcher
github.com/assafelovic/gpt-researcher.what: Active open research agent with planner/executor/publisher separation and configurable recursive breadth/depth research.
github.com/assafelovic/gpt-researcher.lesson: Represent research as a branching question frontier with branch-local context and controlled parallel execution.

github.com/langchain-ai/local-deep-resea.name: langchain-ai/local-deep-researcher
github.com/langchain-ai/local-deep-resea.what: Local LangGraph researcher whose graph explicitly cycles through search, summarization, reflection, knowledge-gap detection, and follow-up query generation; updated August 2025.
github.com/langchain-ai/local-deep-resea.lesson: Make every continuation decision a typed gap-to-query transition rather than an unstructured Next Focus note.

github.com/gair-nlp/deepresearcher.name: GAIR-NLP/DeepResearcher
github.com/gair-nlp/deepresearcher.what: 2025 real-web reinforcement-learning framework reporting emergent planning, source cross-validation, reflective redirection, and honest abstention.
github.com/gair-nlp/deepresearcher.lesson: Require claim-level cross-validation and an explicit unresolved state instead of treating source count as sufficient evidence.

github.com/bytedance/deer-flow.name: bytedance/deer-flow
github.com/bytedance/deer-flow.what: February 2026 ground-up rewrite using a lead agent, scoped parallel subagents, persistent memory, background tasks, and sandboxed artifacts.
github.com/bytedance/deer-flow.lesson: Let a research-plan node spawn a bounded branch with its own context and termination, then merge structured claim deltas rather than prose reports.

github.com/asreview/asreview.name: asreview/asreview
github.com/asreview/asreview.what: Active-learning systematic-review screening with automatically saved decisions, project export, duplicate handling, and v3.0.8 released June 2026.
github.com/asreview/asreview.lesson: Persist screening labels and use their history to prioritize the next evidence candidates instead of relying on an agent's self-reported novelty.

github.com/researchobject/ro-crate-py.name: ResearchObject/ro-crate-py
github.com/researchobject/ro-crate-py.what: Python implementation for creating and consuming RO-Crate 1.2 research-object packages; active release activity continued in July 2026.
github.com/researchobject/ro-crate-py.lesson: Package state, evidence manifests, prompts, activities, and software metadata into a portable research object rather than leaving replay dependent on local paths.

github.com/in-toto/attestation.name: in-toto/attestation
github.com/in-toto/attestation.what: CNCF attestation specification with digest-bound subjects, typed predicates, protobufs, and Go/Python/Rust/Java bindings; v1.2.0 released March 2026.
github.com/in-toto/attestation.lesson: Represent every source capture or synthesis checkpoint as a digest-bound typed statement so provenance can be independently verified.

github.com/imlrz/deepresearch-bench-ii.name: imlrz/DeepResearch-Bench-II
github.com/imlrz/deepresearch-bench-ii.what: 2026 deep-research evaluation pipeline with 132 expert-derived tasks, 9,430 fine-grained rubrics, JSONL results, and separate recall, analysis, and presentation aggregation.
github.com/imlrz/deepresearch-bench-ii.lesson: Reuse its hierarchical rubric structure but extend evaluation from terminal reports to replayability, claim provenance, update precision, and affected-claim detection.

github.com/markrussinovich/refchecker.name: markrussinovich/refchecker
github.com/markrussinovich/refchecker.what: Bibliographic validator using deterministic multi-index checks, LLM search escalation, and metadata reverification; repository page was live in June 2026.
github.com/markrussinovich/refchecker.lesson: Detect fabricated sources cheaply before judging claim semantics, and never trust metadata produced by the escalation model without deterministic reverification.

github.com/amazon-science/refchecker.name: amazon-science/RefChecker
github.com/amazon-science/refchecker.what: Three-stage claim-triplet extraction, entailment checking, aggregation, and evidence localization; README news was last visibly dated July 2024.
github.com/amazon-science/refchecker.lesson: Admit findings at atomic-claim level with entailment, neutral, contradiction, and abstention states rather than trusting iteration-level confidence.

github.com/amazon-science/ragchecker.name: amazon-science/RAGChecker
github.com/amazon-science/ragchecker.what: Claim-level diagnostic framework separating answer correctness, completeness, and faithfulness to retrieved context; repository page was live in May 2026.
github.com/amazon-science/ragchecker.lesson: Separate retrieval failure from synthesis failure so convergence cannot hide missing evidence behind fluent generation.

github.com/microsoft/bipia.name: microsoft/BIPIA
github.com/microsoft/bipia.what: Indirect-prompt-injection benchmark covering Web QA, email, tables, summarization, and code QA; repository page was live in June 2026.
github.com/microsoft/bipia.lesson: Treat retrieved-content poisoning as an evidence-admission failure and test it with generated attacked contexts, not prompt wording alone.

github.com/future-house/paper-qa.name: Future-House/paper-qa
github.com/future-house/paper-qa.what: Scientific RAG with query-conditioned evidence gathering, passage scoring, contradiction search, and citation-linked answers; README documents substantial December 2025 updates.
github.com/future-house/paper-qa.lesson: Separate evidence acquisition from prose synthesis and expose support and contradiction retrieval as explicit claim-level operations.

github.com/colrev-environment/colrev.name: CoLRev-Environment/colrev
github.com/colrev-environment/colrev.what: Git-backed collaborative literature-review environment; current documentation reports version 0.16.2 with validate, undo, curation, and explicit workflow stages.
github.com/colrev-environment/colrev.lesson: Model research synthesis as reversible typed stages with validation rather than an accumulating prose document.

github.com/trungdong/prov.name: trungdong/prov
github.com/trungdong/prov.what: Python implementation of W3C PROV with PROV-JSON, PROV-O/RDF, graph export, and release 2.1.1 dated 2025-06-24.
github.com/trungdong/prov.lesson: Represent claims, evidence spans, iterations, derivations, and invalidations with an established provenance model instead of inventing incompatible lineage fields.

github.com/ourresearch/openalex-docs.name: ourresearch/openalex-docs
github.com/ourresearch/openalex-docs.what: Official OpenAlex API documentation containing work retraction state and incremental from_updated_date filtering; documentation was current when crawled in 2026.
github.com/ourresearch/openalex-docs.lesson: Maintain a source-refresh watermark and use metadata changes to enqueue targeted claim revalidation rather than rerunning the entire research topic.

github.com/the-pr-agent/pr-agent.name: The-PR-Agent/pr-agent
github.com/the-pr-agent/pr-agent.what: Open-source legacy PR reviewer; release v0.39.0 published July 5, 2026, with dynamic context, token-aware compression, and suggestion self-reflection.
github.com/the-pr-agent/pr-agent.lesson: pr_agent/tools/pr_code_suggestions.py and its documented second-pass 0-10 scoring show a concrete candidate re-ranking stage, while dynamic context expands hunks toward enclosing functions/classes instead of blindly adding lines.

github.com/semgrep/semgrep.name: semgrep/semgrep
github.com/semgrep/semgrep.what: Deterministic multi-language static-analysis engine; v1.169.0 released July 8, 2026.
github.com/semgrep/semgrep.lesson: Use deterministic analyzers as high-recall candidate producers before LLM adjudication; Semgrep Assistant's commercial scoped-memory layer is documented separately and is not implemented in this repository.

github.com/swe-bench/swe-smith.name: SWE-bench/SWE-smith
github.com/swe-bench/swe-smith.what: NeurIPS 2025 Spotlight toolkit that builds Dockerized repository environments and synthesizes test-breaking software-engineering tasks.
github.com/swe-bench/swe-smith.lesson: Its RepoProfile/get_container abstraction and keep-only-if-a-test-breaks filter are transferable to isolated base/head reproduction receipts for review findings.

github.com/withmartian/code-review-bench.name: withmartian/code-review-benchmark
github.com/withmartian/code-review-bench.what: 2026 open benchmark with fixed human-curated PR issues plus a continuously refreshed online benchmark of bot comments and subsequent developer actions.
github.com/withmartian/code-review-bench.lesson: Adopt its offline/online cross-check, explicit candidate dedup stage, per-judge result storage, and precision/recall stratification instead of optimizing review convergence alone.

github.com/purcl/repoaudit.name: PurCL/RepoAudit
github.com/purcl/repoaudit.what: Neuro-symbolic repository auditor with parser-backed control-flow checks and a separate path-feasibility validator; paper revised May 2025 and architecture updated July 2025.
github.com/purcl/repoaudit.lesson: Represent a finding as a path-sensitive hypothesis, then validate its intermediate facts and path satisfiability before publication.

github.com/adamjgmiller/adamsreview.name: adamjgmiller/adamsreview
github.com/adamjgmiller/adamsreview.what: Active multi-lens review pipeline with 229 commits, current v0.4.0, persistent artifacts, cheap/deep validation lanes, re-review, and regression reversion.
github.com/adamjgmiller/adamsreview.lesson: Use a typed finding lifecycle with source families, score history, validation dispositions, blast radius, fix attempts, SHA freshness, and separate human promotion.

github.com/stryker-mutator/stryker-js.name: stryker-mutator/stryker-js
github.com/stryker-mutator/stryker-js.what: Production mutation-testing monorepo; v9.6.1 released April 10, 2026, with incremental and exact line-range mutation support.
github.com/stryker-mutator/stryker-js.lesson: Turn severe review claims into diff-scoped challenge experiments and reuse content-addressed results across passes.

github.com/reviewdog/reviewdog.name: reviewdog/reviewdog
github.com/reviewdog/reviewdog.what: Language-agnostic review diagnostic router; latest tagged release observed v0.21.0 in September 2025.
github.com/reviewdog/reviewdog.lesson: Preserve all deterministic diagnostics but separately track diff relevance, source context, and reportability before escalating residuals to judges.

github.com/github/codeql-action.name: github/codeql-action
github.com/github/codeql-action.what: CodeQL analysis and SARIF upload implementation; CodeQL Bundle v2.26.0 released July 8, 2026.
github.com/github/codeql-action.lesson: Use normalized rolling source-context fingerprints and occurrence disambiguation instead of absolute lines for cross-revision finding identity.

github.com/microsoft/sarif-sdk.name: microsoft/sarif-sdk
github.com/microsoft/sarif-sdk.what: SARIF object model, validation tooling, and recent AI-finding profile; v4.6.5 released May 18, 2026.
github.com/microsoft/sarif-sdk.lesson: Separate severity, confidence, exploitability, evidence strength and scope; assign durable fingerprints in the reducer rather than the AI producer.

github.com/hcoles/pitest.name: hcoles/pitest
github.com/hcoles/pitest.what: JVM mutation-testing and verification system; v1.25.7 released July 8, 2026.
github.com/hcoles/pitest.lesson: Turn accepted findings into mutation or reproduction obligations and gate later fixes on pre-fix failure, post-fix success, and non-regressing test strength.

github.com/crytic/slither.name: crytic/slither
github.com/crytic/slither.what: Active Solidity/Vyper analyzer; release 0.11.5 appeared in January 2026 and detectors declare impact and confidence independently.
github.com/crytic/slither.lesson: Make severity and epistemic confidence orthogonal required fields; a high-impact, low-confidence candidate routes to verification rather than immediately blocking.

github.com/rust-lang/rust-clippy.name: rust-lang/rust-clippy
github.com/rust-lang/rust-clippy.what: Actively maintained Rust linter with UI golden tests, lintcheck runs over real crates, graduated lint groups, and fix-applicability metadata.
github.com/rust-lang/rust-clippy.lesson: Audit reviewer-policy changes on a fixed project corpus, quarantine immature checks, and distinguish safe executable corrections from possibly incorrect suggestions.

github.com/sonarsource/sonarqube.name: SonarSource/sonarqube
github.com/sonarsource/sonarqube.what: Continuous-analysis platform with current 2026.1 lifecycle semantics including open, accepted, false-positive, fixed, and sandboxed issues.
github.com/sonarsource/sonarqube.lesson: Give new model/prompt/rule digests a non-blocking probation state and preserve accepted-risk separately from false-positive feedback.

github.com/foundryhq-ai/swe-prbench.name: FoundryHQ-AI/swe-prbench
github.com/foundryhq-ai/swe-prbench.what: March 2026 evaluation harness with frozen rubric, versioned pipeline, three context configurations, raw outputs, judge outputs, and validation-failure artifacts.
github.com/foundryhq-ai/swe-prbench.lesson: Track review quality by direct, contextual, and latent issue distance; treat context composition as a controlled intervention rather than monotonically useful input.

github.com/kaustpradalab/llm-sycophancy.name: kaustpradalab/LLM-sycophancy
github.com/kaustpradalab/llm-sycophancy.what: AAAI 2026 experiment suite with paired plain, opinion, expertise, and first-person versus third-person prompt datasets.
github.com/kaustpradalab/llm-sycophancy.lesson: Run paired author-framing controls and measure unsupported verdict or severity flips; normalize primary review inputs to third-person facts.

github.com/facebook/infer.name: facebook/infer
github.com/facebook/infer.what: Compositional static analyzer; v1.3.0 released May 2026 added equality-saturation Semdiff and strengthened incremental analysis.
github.com/facebook/infer.lesson: Normalize semantically irrelevant changes before review and retain introduced/fixed/preexisting issue lineage across patch revisions.

github.com/joernio/joern.name: joernio/joern
github.com/joernio/joern.what: Cross-language code-property-graph platform with interprocedural data-flow slicing and a July 2026 release.
github.com/joernio/joern.lesson: Define review coverage as JSON program slices from changed nodes to callers, data-flow sources, and observable sinks rather than changed files.

github.com/wilfred/difftastic.name: Wilfred/difftastic
github.com/wilfred/difftastic.what: Tree-sitter structural diff using graph search; v0.69.0 released April 2026.
github.com/wilfred/difftastic.lesson: Use AST equality as a cheap first-stage noise filter, while preserving a conservative raw-diff fallback on parse errors or unsupported languages.

github.com/assert-kth/collector-sahab.name: ASSERT-KTH/collector-sahab
github.com/assert-kth/collector-sahab.what: Before/after Java runtime-state differencer split into matched-line, trace-collection, and trace-diff modules; latest release September 2024.
github.com/assert-kth/collector-sahab.lesson: Attach concise runtime witnesses to review claims, but filter unstable and causally unrelated values before using them as severity evidence.

github.com/diffkemp/diffkemp.name: diffkemp/diffkemp
github.com/diffkemp/diffkemp.what: LLVM-based semantic-equivalence comparison for selected functions in large C projects; latest release August 2024.
github.com/diffkemp/diffkemp.lesson: Escalate high-risk refactor obligations to selective before/after equivalence checks and report unsupported transformations as residual risk, not equality.

github.com/spacehunterinf/dmad.name: SpaceHunterInf/DMAD
github.com/spacehunterinf/dmad.what: Official 2026 implementation of diversity-aware initialization and confidence-modulated debate; repository currently has 5 commits.
github.com/spacehunterinf/dmad.lesson: Oversample initial answers, select a maximally diverse subset, and distinguish confidence expression from the ability to use confidence during revision.

github.com/tmlr-group/econ.name: tmlr-group/ECON
github.com/tmlr-group/econ.what: ICML 2025 belief-driven coordination implementation with 17 commits, learned belief states, hierarchical coordinators, BNE refinement, and oscillation guards.
github.com/tmlr-group/econ.lesson: Represent each seat's belief and commitment explicitly; borrow equilibrium and oscillation checks without making RL training mandatory.

github.com/algorithmicsuperintelligence/.name: algorithmicsuperintelligence/optillm
github.com/algorithmicsuperintelligence/.what: Actively maintained inference proxy; release v0.3.21 was published July 5, 2026 and its moa.py implements same-model candidate, critique, and synthesis.
github.com/algorithmicsuperintelligence/.lesson: Use its same-model MoA path as a mandatory control arm that the council must beat, not as evidence that multiple seats are intrinsically valuable.

github.com/dayeonki/cultural_debate.name: dayeonki/cultural_debate
github.com/dayeonki/cultural_debate.what: ACL 2025 Oral code and data with 15 commits, heterogeneous model pairings, country-specific evidence, and accuracy/parity evaluation.
github.com/dayeonki/cultural_debate.lesson: For plural-value decisions, evaluate group parity and contextual legitimacy alongside aggregate correctness and preserve non-majority positions.

github.com/deeplearning-wisc/mad-identit.name: deeplearning-wisc/MAD-identity-bias
github.com/deeplearning-wisc/mad-identit.what: New official ACL 2026 Oral code with vanilla/anonymized runs, JSONL histories, and IBC computation in debate/metrics.py.
github.com/deeplearning-wisc/mad-identit.lesson: Blind response provenance and measure conformity-versus-obstinacy instead of assuming named seats deliberate impartially.

github.com/multi-agent-llms/mallm.name: Multi-Agent-LLMs/mallm
github.com/multi-agent-llms/mallm.what: Separates mallm/agents, mallm/discourse_policy, and mallm/decision_protocol; latest release v1.0.6 was June 15, 2026.
github.com/multi-agent-llms/mallm.lesson: Make voting, consensus, judge, report, relay, and debate policies independently selectable and benchmarkable rather than coupling deliberation to two-of-three convergence.

github.com/lkaesberg/decision-protocols.name: lkaesberg/decision-protocols
github.com/lkaesberg/decision-protocols.what: Controlled ACL 2025 experiment configurations isolate decision protocol and independent-drafting effects using MALLM.
github.com/lkaesberg/decision-protocols.lesson: Select aggregation by task class: voting was stronger for reasoning, consensus for knowledge, while independent drafting improved answer diversity.

github.com/mradonkey/dmad.name: MraDonkey/DMAD
github.com/mradonkey/dmad.what: ICLR 2025 implementation uses main_DMAD.py, prompt.py, reasoning.py, and record.py to assign distinct reasoning strategies.
github.com/mradonkey/dmad.lesson: Diversify cognitive procedures such as IO, compositional CoT, and duty-distinct CoT; persona labels alone do not break a shared mental set.

github.com/lucasmaystre/choix.name: lucasmaystre/choix
github.com/lucasmaystre/choix.what: Bradley-Terry and Plackett-Luce inference library with ML, spectral, Rank Centrality, and Bayesian expectation-propagation APIs; latest repository release shown in August 2025.
github.com/lucasmaystre/choix.lesson: Use ep_pairwise to turn blinded council comparisons into posterior means and covariance rather than majority rankings.

github.com/pyro-ppl/pyro.name: pyro-ppl/pyro
github.com/pyro-ppl/pyro.what: Probabilistic-programming system whose pyro.contrib.oed.eig module implements several expected-information-gain estimators; latest tagged release shown is 1.9.1 from June 2024, with an active development branch.
github.com/pyro-ppl/pyro.lesson: Represent seat reliability, provider correlation, item difficulty, and seat-selection utility in one posterior model, then select the next seat by information gain per cost.

github.com/arjunpanickssery/math_problem.name: ArjunPanickssery/math_problems_debate
github.com/arjunpanickssery/math_problem.what: Scalable-oversight debate and consultancy experiment code with 419 commits, deterministic seeded randomness, cached LLM calls, simulated cost preflight, tests, and JSONL cost accounting.
github.com/arjunpanickssery/math_problem.lesson: Make debate a reproducible protocol object with explicit consultancy and direct-answer controls, not an unstructured extra conversation round.

github.com/ncsoft/offsetbias.name: ncsoft/offsetbias
github.com/ncsoft/offsetbias.what: EMNLP 2024 reference code and EvalBiasBench data for pairwise judges exposed to length, concreteness, empty-reference, continuation, nested-instruction, and familiar-knowledge traps.
github.com/ncsoft/offsetbias.lesson: Promote judge-bias tests from an offline benchmark into packet-level metamorphic gates before a council verdict is accepted.

github.com/snumprlab/lwe.name: snumprlab/lwe
github.com/snumprlab/lwe.what: EACL 2026 implementation of fixed, sample-specific, evolving, and selectively evolving judge prompts with swap consistency and pair-accuracy outputs.
github.com/snumprlab/lwe.lesson: Use self-inconsistency to target judge calibration work, but freeze and version the resulting judge state before live packet adjudication.

github.com/zhiyuan-weng/benchform.name: zhiyuan-weng/BenchForm
github.com/zhiyuan-weng/benchform.what: ICLR 2025 conformity benchmark with Raw, Correct Guidance, Wrong Guidance, Trust, and Doubt protocols; repository recency could not be verified beyond the 2025 publication.
github.com/zhiyuan-weng/benchform.lesson: Turn false-consensus risks into controlled council test fixtures that distinguish immediate majority pressure from history-conditioned trust and doubt.

github.com/aialt/sos-llms.name: aialt/SoS-LLMs
github.com/aialt/sos-llms.what: Code and data for the EMNLP 2025 Spiral-of-Silence study; current GitHub page shows 2 commits with inference and analysis directories.
github.com/aialt/sos-llms.lesson: Monitor opinion trajectories and concentration over rounds, because history-driven anchoring and minority disappearance are longitudinal failures invisible to final-answer similarity.

github.com/ahren09/agentreview.name: Ahren09/AgentReview
github.com/ahren09/agentreview.what: Factorial multi-agent peer-review simulator; latest visible commit is May 2026, with configurable traits in agentreview/experiment_config.py and staged deliberation in paper_review_arena.py.
github.com/ahren09/agentreview.lesson: Test one social variable at a time—malicious seat, reviewer commitment, anonymous versus known identity, conformist versus inclusive adjudicator—and compare outcome deltas to a fixed baseline.

github.com/compdemocracy/polis.name: compdemocracy/polis
github.com/compdemocracy/polis.what: Production deliberation platform with roughly 9.3k commits and 100+ visible open pull requests; mathematical processing lives under delphi/polismath and the legacy math service.
github.com/compdemocracy/polis.lesson: Aggregate a claim-by-seat agree/disagree/uncertain matrix and preserve group-informed dissent instead of collapsing deliberation into one majority score.

github.com/oakleyj/shelf.name: OakleyJ/SHELF
github.com/oakleyj/shelf.what: Structured expert-elicitation package with fitdist/feedback workflows, group distribution pooling, and an April 2026 v1.13.0 release.
github.com/oakleyj/shelf.lesson: Represent factual seat judgments as distributions with private estimates and feedback, rather than reducing them immediately to prose confidence scores.

github.com/voting-tools/pref_voting.name: voting-tools/pref_voting
github.com/voting-tools/pref_voting.what: JOSS-published Python social-choice toolkit; profiles, explicit ties, pairwise margins, weighted-majority graphs, and Split Cycle live in dedicated modules; first release January 2025.
github.com/voting-tools/pref_voting.lesson: Aggregate council option preferences as a pairwise graph and preserve cycles or unbeaten sets instead of forcing every disagreement through one scalar score.

github.com/argdown/argdown.name: argdown/argdown
github.com/argdown/argdown.what: Parseable argument-map syntax with core parser, CLI, language server, map views, and Markdown integration; latest tagged release is February 2022, although current main uses modern Node tooling.
github.com/argdown/argdown.lesson: Give council claims, supports, attacks, objections, and revisions stable identifiers and a machine-parseable representation while retaining readable Markdown.

github.com/yoheinakajima/activegraph.name: yoheinakajima/activegraph
github.com/yoheinakajima/activegraph.what: Event-sourced typed graph with fork-and-diff, cached replay, recorded fixtures, and a July 2026 v1.9.0 release.
github.com/yoheinakajima/activegraph.lesson: Fork a council immediately before social exposure, apply one controlled perturbation, and structurally diff claims, preference edges, objections, and verdicts.

github.com/gepa-ai/gepa.name: gepa-ai/gepa
github.com/gepa-ai/gepa.what: Reflective text evolution with case-wise Pareto selection, trace-derived feedback, merge, and adapters; v0.1.3 released 2026-07-14.
github.com/gepa-ai/gepa.lesson: Keep rich evaluator artifacts and per-case fitness vectors, using minibatch admission before expensive full validation.

github.com/algorithmicsuperintelligence/.name: algorithmicsuperintelligence/openevolve
github.com/algorithmicsuperintelligence/.what: Community AlphaEvolve implementation with MAP-Elites, islands, migration, cascaded evaluation, and artifact feedback; v0.3.1 released 2026-07-14.
github.com/algorithmicsuperintelligence/.lesson: Separate performance elites from inspiration candidates and preserve quality-diverse lineages instead of repeatedly mutating one winner.

github.com/stanford-iris-lab/meta-harnes.name: stanford-iris-lab/meta-harness
github.com/stanford-iris-lab/meta-harnes.what: 2026 reference implementation for searching complete model harness code from prior source, scores, and traces.
github.com/stanford-iris-lab/meta-harnes.lesson: Make prompts, skills, memory policy, retrieval, and control code typed mutation surfaces while preserving complete experiment history.

github.com/a-evo-lab/adaptiveharness.name: A-EVO-Lab/AdaptiveHarness
github.com/a-evo-lab/adaptiveharness.what: June 2026 paper-code mirror for harness-tree construction and per-task solve-time routing; canonical development is an A-Evolve release branch.
github.com/a-evo-lab/adaptiveharness.lesson: Promote specialists conditionally by workload profile instead of forcing every accepted improvement into one global harness.

github.com/shengranhu/adas.name: ShengranHu/ADAS
github.com/shengranhu/adas.what: ICLR 2025 Meta Agent Search implementation that iteratively programs complete agent systems from an archive of prior discoveries.
github.com/shengranhu/adas.lesson: Search over agent architecture and reusable building blocks, not only prompt wording, while evaluating generated systems through a fixed task interface.

github.com/xianglinyang/llm-as-a-judge-a.name: xianglinyang/llm-as-a-judge-attack
github.com/xianglinyang/llm-as-a-judge-a.what: May 2026 BITE implementation; src/evolve_agent/bandit/UCB performs budgeted black-box judge exploitation, with transferability and detection notebooks.
github.com/xianglinyang/llm-as-a-judge-a.lesson: Treat exact evaluator feedback as an attack surface: budget hidden-eval queries and run semantics-preserving score-inflation attacks before promotion.

github.com/tlc4418/llm_optimization.name: tlc4418/llm_optimization
github.com/tlc4418/llm_optimization.what: ICLR 2024 reference code with six commits and no releases; src/bon/run_bon_pipeline.py and PPO configs implement individual, mean, WCO, and UWO reward-model evaluation.
github.com/tlc4418/llm_optimization.lesson: Fan out to diverse evaluators and promote on a conservative lower bound or uncertainty-weighted objective, not one judge or an unqualified mean.

github.com/argoproj/argo-rollouts.name: argoproj/argo-rollouts
github.com/argoproj/argo-rollouts.what: Active progressive-delivery controller; v1.9.0 released March 20, 2026, with AnalysisTemplate, Experiment, traffic mirroring, pauses, inconclusive states, promotion, and abort.
github.com/argoproj/argo-rollouts.lesson: Extend accept/ship into shadow/canary/ship stages whose analysis receipts can pause, promote, or restore the stable baseline automatically.

github.com/giskard-ai/giskard-oss.name: Giskard-AI/giskard-oss
github.com/giskard-ai/giskard-oss.what: Active agent-evaluation repository; giskard-scan v1.0.0b3 released July 13, 2026, with Scenario checks, multi-turn testing, vulnerability_scan, and an extensible scenario-generator registry.
github.com/giskard-ai/giskard-oss.lesson: Generate adversarial evaluator canaries from the target description, validate them against known-good and known-bad outputs, then freeze that generated suite for the evaluation epoch.

github.com/microsoft/waza.name: microsoft/waza
github.com/microsoft/waza.what: Agent-skill evaluation framework with captured workspace/session evidence, deterministic and prompt graders, adversarial packs, snapshots, and a v0.38.0 release in June 2026.
github.com/microsoft/waza.lesson: Give frozen graders a narrow interface over captured run receipts rather than access to mutable candidate execution state.

github.com/braintrustdata/autoevals.name: braintrustdata/autoevals
github.com/braintrustdata/autoevals.what: Evaluator library whose scorer results separate normalized scores from raw metadata and rationale; JavaScript v0.3.0 released in June 2026.
github.com/braintrustdata/autoevals.lesson: Persist raw evaluator observations independently from versioned normalization and score reduction.

github.com/confident-ai/deepeval.name: confident-ai/deepeval
github.com/confident-ai/deepeval.what: Evaluation framework with ArenaGEval candidate masking and randomized comparison order; Python 4.1.0 released in July 2026.
github.com/confident-ai/deepeval.lesson: Use salted candidate aliases and order-swapped pairwise judging to reduce identity and position bias during promotion.

github.com/beeevita/evoprompt.name: beeevita/EvoPrompt
github.com/beeevita/evoprompt.what: ICLR 2024 reference implementation of GA and differential-evolution prompt optimization with explicit crossover, mutation, parent selection, and incumbent replacement.
github.com/beeevita/evoprompt.lesson: Adopt evolutionary candidate operators but replace scalar top-k survival with five-dimensional Pareto or lexicase selection.

github.com/steven-b-cho/llmorph.name: steven-b-cho/llmorph
github.com/steven-b-cho/llmorph.what: ICSME 2025 metamorphic-testing artifact implementing 36 relations and retaining paired source/follow-up executions; paper updated in 2026.
github.com/steven-b-cho/llmorph.lesson: Represent robustness checks as executable precondition-transform-oracle relations with paired immutable receipts.

github.com/anthropics/sycophancy-to-subt.name: anthropics/sycophancy-to-subterfuge-paper
github.com/anthropics/sycophancy-to-subt.what: 2024 reward-tampering artifacts, archived June 2025; environments.json separates reward_cmd from oversight_cmd and publishes tampering samples.
github.com/anthropics/sycophancy-to-subt.lesson: Optimization reward and tamper oversight must be independent channels; successful task reward cannot itself authorize promotion.

github.com/livebench/livebench.name: LiveBench/LiveBench
github.com/livebench/livebench.what: ICLR 2025 benchmark with objective ground-truth scoring, rotating questions, and a changelog updated January 2026.
github.com/livebench/livebench.lesson: Use versioned canary epochs that rotate before repeated adaptive evaluation turns them into training data; prefer deterministic ground truth over judge opinion.

github.com/lm-sys/llm-decontaminator.name: lm-sys/llm-decontaminator
github.com/lm-sys/llm-decontaminator.what: 2023 contamination detector using embedding top-k retrieval in vector_db.py followed by LLM semantic-equivalence classification in llm_detect.py; no releases.
github.com/lm-sys/llm-decontaminator.lesson: Scan every proposer-visible artifact for paraphrased canary leakage inside the evaluator boundary, returning only a leak veto rather than the matching text.

github.com/seldonio/alibi-detect.name: SeldonIO/alibi-detect
github.com/seldonio/alibi-detect.what: Outlier, adversarial, and drift detection library; v0.13.0 released December 2025, including MMD, KS, classifier, and online detectors.
github.com/seldonio/alibi-detect.lesson: Compare baseline and candidate action-trace distributions, not only final scores; unexplained semantic or tool-use drift is evidence of capability drift.

github.com/deepchecks/deepchecks.name: deepchecks/deepchecks
github.com/deepchecks/deepchecks.what: Continuous model/data validation library; v0.19.1 released December 2024, with WeakSegmentsPerformance and configurable pass/fail conditions.
github.com/deepchecks/deepchecks.lesson: Train shallow error-prediction trees over fixture metadata to discover concentrated regressions that aggregate promotion scores conceal.

github.com/nvidia-nemo/evaluator.name: NVIDIA-NeMo/Evaluator
github.com/nvidia-nemo/evaluator.what: Reproducible evaluation framework with paired run comparison, policy-driven GO/NO-GO/INCONCLUSIVE gates, interceptors, sandboxes, and an April 2026 release signal.
github.com/nvidia-nemo/evaluator.lesson: Adapt nemo_evaluator.engine.gate's per-item paired evidence and critical/supporting/advisory policy tiers for candidate promotion, keeping INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE distinct from PASS.

github.com/hypothesisworks/hypothesis.name: HypothesisWorks/hypothesis
github.com/hypothesisworks/hypothesis.what: Property-based and stateful testing library with generation, targeted search, failure replay, and automatic shrinking; release 6.156.6 shipped July 10, 2026.
github.com/hypothesisworks/hypothesis.lesson: Represent metamorphic fixture families as strategies, target high-severity relation violations, and shrink failures into minimal promotion-blocking counterexamples.

github.com/microsoft/test_awareness_stee.name: microsoft/Test_Awareness_Steering
github.com/microsoft/test_awareness_stee.what: NeurIPS 2025 research code and paired real-versus-hypothetical datasets for measuring evaluation awareness; no releases found.
github.com/microsoft/test_awareness_stee.lesson: Add black-box evaluation-context twins that differ only in realistic versus obvious test cues and veto candidates whose behavior becomes more cue-dependent.

github.com/treeverse/dvc.name: treeverse/dvc
github.com/treeverse/dvc.what: Data and experiment versioning system whose pipeline metadata links code, parameters, dependencies, outputs, and cached executions; release 3.67.1 shipped March 31, 2026.
github.com/treeverse/dvc.lesson: Use a dependency-lock-shaped evaluator capsule so profile, fixtures, grader configuration, calibration, and environment form one versioned evaluation epoch; retain SHA-256 rather than copying DVC hash defaults.

github.com/open-policy-agent/opa.name: open-policy-agent/opa
github.com/open-policy-agent/opa.what: General policy compiler/evaluator; the release page showed v1.17.0 on 2026-05-28 with rule labels and published IR/bundle schemas.
github.com/open-policy-agent/opa.lesson: Compile, type-check, capability-check, test, coverage-gate, revision, sign, and label an authority capsule before using it for findings.

github.com/kyverno/kyverno.name: kyverno/kyverno
github.com/kyverno/kyverno.what: Kubernetes policy platform; CEL policy types arrived in 2025 and became stable in v1.18, released in 2026.
github.com/kyverno/kyverno.lesson: Represent deviations as scoped policy-referencing objects and preserve pass/fail/warn/error/skip outcomes in background audit reports.

github.com/microsoft/typespec.name: microsoft/typespec
github.com/microsoft/typespec.what: Typed specification compiler with reusable libraries, linter rulesets, emitters, code fixes, and rule testers; v1.14.0 released 2026-07-14.
github.com/microsoft/typespec.lesson: Compile formalizable authority prose into a typed rule IR and expose residual ambiguous obligations as reasoning-required rather than silently omitting them.

github.com/specmatic/specmatic.name: specmatic/specmatic
github.com/specmatic/specmatic.what: Executable-contract and compatibility system; release 2.50.0 published 2026-07-06.
github.com/specmatic/specmatic.lesson: Replay old-authority witnesses against a new authority capsule, validate historical examples, then verify live artifacts to detect semantic weakening or breaking changes.

github.com/schemathesis/schemathesis.name: schemathesis/schemathesis
github.com/schemathesis/schemathesis.what: Schema-driven positive, negative, boundary, fuzz, and stateful conformance testing; release 4.22.4 published 2026-07-08.
github.com/schemathesis/schemathesis.lesson: Generate rule witnesses, explore relational behavior, shrink failures, and retain deterministic replay cases instead of recording prose-only findings.

github.com/usnistgov/oscal.name: usnistgov/OSCAL
github.com/usnistgov/oscal.what: NIST's machine-readable control, implementation, assessment-plan, observation, evidence, and finding models; v1.2.2 released 2026-04-30.
github.com/usnistgov/oscal.lesson: Represent conformance as a typed chain from authority and assessment scope through observations and relevant evidence to findings, rather than as standalone warnings.

github.com/openscap/openscap.name: OpenSCAP/openscap
github.com/openscap/openscap.what: NIST-certified SCAP evaluator using XCCDF profiles, OVAL checks, CPE applicability, and machine-readable results; v1.4.4 released 2026-04-09.
github.com/openscap/openscap.lesson: Resolve profile and target applicability before evaluation, and preserve not-applicable as a first-class outcome instead of coercing it to pass or fail.

github.com/complianceascode/content.name: ComplianceAsCode/content
github.com/complianceascode/content.what: Authority-referenced YAML rules and profiles compiled into SCAP, OVAL, Ansible, Bash, and CEL; release 0.1.81 published 2026-06-01.
github.com/complianceascode/content.lesson: Separate canonical rule metadata, authority identifiers, platform applicability, executable checks, and output formats so authority fidelity can be tested independently of execution.

github.com/inspec/inspec.name: inspec/inspec
github.com/inspec/inspec.what: Infrastructure compliance testing framework with control-scoped waivers, justifications, optional expiry, and run-versus-skip behavior; v5.24.24 released 2026-06-25.
github.com/inspec/inspec.lesson: A known deviation should normally continue executing and change the disposition of a reproduced failure; skipping verification destroys drift visibility.

github.com/openvex/vexctl.name: openvex/vexctl
github.com/openvex/vexctl.what: Creates, merges, signs, replays, and applies versioned OpenVEX negative assertions; v0.4.4 released 2026-06-16.
github.com/openvex/vexctl.lesson: Model suppression as a precise, versioned negative assertion bound to subject, issue, status, timestamp, justification, and issuer—not as an ignored identifier.

github.com/cel-expr/cel-go.name: cel-expr/cel-go
github.com/cel-expr/cel-go.what: Typed, non-Turing-complete expression engine with source diagnostics, residual unknown expressions, and cost tracking; v0.29.2 released 2026-07-08.
github.com/cel-expr/cel-go.lesson: Compile authority rules into checked, source-linked executable predicates while retaining unknown inputs instead of forcing pass/fail.

github.com/bufbuild/buf.name: bufbuild/buf
github.com/bufbuild/buf.what: Schema compatibility engine with several breaking-change categories, scoped policies, exceptions, and a large compatibility corpus; v1.71.0 released 2026-06-16.
github.com/bufbuild/buf.lesson: Represent authority drift with explicit compatibility classes and replay only rules and artifacts affected by the changed contract.

github.com/oss-review-toolkit/ort.name: oss-review-toolkit/ort
github.com/oss-review-toolkit/ort.what: Policy evaluator with typed resolutions kept separate from analyzer and scanner evidence; release 91.0.1 published 2026-07-09.
github.com/oss-review-toolkit/ort.lesson: Apply known deviations as an adjudication overlay without deleting the raw finding or observation history.

github.com/sigstore/cosign.name: sigstore/cosign
github.com/sigstore/cosign.what: Artifact verification binds digests, signer identity, certificates, signatures, and transparency evidence; v3.1.1 released 2026-06-09.
github.com/sigstore/cosign.lesson: Make every conformance assertion depend on a receipt binding subject, authority epoch, verifier version, and evidence inputs.

github.com/theupdateframework/python-tuf.name: theupdateframework/python-tuf
github.com/theupdateframework/python-tuf.what: Python TUF reference implementation; v7.0.0 released 2026-05-18.
github.com/theupdateframework/python-tuf.lesson: Pin an authority capsule and reject expired, rolled-back, or mixed-version authority material before auditing artifacts.

github.com/aquasecurity/trivy.name: aquasecurity/trivy
github.com/aquasecurity/trivy.lesson: Known deviations should be rule-specific, path-scoped, justified, and expiring rather than permanent blanket suppressions.

github.com/yelp/detect-secrets.name: Yelp/detect-secrets
github.com/yelp/detect-secrets.what: Baseline-oriented secret scanner; latest tagged release v1.5.0 was 2024-05-06.
github.com/yelp/detect-secrets.lesson: Baselines can freeze an obsolete detector set; explicitly re-run latest verifiers and separately scan allowlisted material.

github.com/github/codeql.name: github/codeql
github.com/github/codeql.what: Open CodeQL query and library packs; active in 2026 with bundle v2.25.5 shipped in May.
github.com/github/codeql.lesson: Give rules stable lineage and precision classes, then separate blocking high-precision checks from recall-oriented candidate suites.

github.com/cyclonedx/specification.name: CycloneDX/specification
github.com/cyclonedx/specification.what: ECMA-424 reference schemas; v1.7 shipped October 2025 and models standards declarations, claims, counterclaims, evidence, assessors, confidence, and signatures.
github.com/cyclonedx/specification.lesson: Use a requirement-to-claim-and-counterevidence certificate model, but preserve discrete normative blocking rather than reducing alignment to a score.

github.com/slsa-framework/slsa-verifier.name: slsa-framework/slsa-verifier
github.com/slsa-framework/slsa-verifier.what: Provenance and Verification Summary Attestation verifier; latest located release v2.7.1 was June 2025.
github.com/slsa-framework/slsa-verifier.lesson: Bind every alignment conclusion to immutable subject digests, expected authority/source identity, verifier identity, and explicit verified levels; reject mutable-reference replay.

github.com/alan-turing-institute/assuran.name: alan-turing-institute/AssurancePlatform
github.com/alan-turing-institute/assuran.what: Active research-preview platform for structured claim-strategy-evidence assurance cases; release 0.4.1 dated March 12, 2026.
github.com/alan-turing-institute/assuran.lesson: Represent alignment as a navigable claim/evidence graph with typed links and history, enabling targeted invalidation and stakeholder review instead of flat findings.

github.com/ibm/prompt-declaration-langua.name: IBM/prompt-declaration-language
github.com/ibm/prompt-declaration-langua.what: Declarative prompt-program runtime with AutoPDL source-to-source optimization; v0.9.3 released February 2026.
github.com/ibm/prompt-declaration-langua.lesson: Represent models, demonstrations, verification calls, and prompting patterns as typed free variables, then use successive halving and emit an executable optimized artifact.

github.com/evoagentx/evoagentx.name: EvoAgentX/EvoAgentX
github.com/evoagentx/evoagentx.what: Framework integrating TextGrad, AFlow, MIPRO, EvoPrompt, MAP-Elites, workflow evaluation, rollback, and human checkpoints; v0.1.4 released June 2026.
github.com/evoagentx/evoagentx.lesson: Expose multiple optimizer backends behind one agent-workflow IR and distinguish prompt-only optimization from topology and operator search.

github.com/aiwaves-cn/agents.name: aiwaves-cn/agents
github.com/aiwaves-cn/agents.what: Agents 2.0 implementation of symbolic learning over prompts, tools, nodes, and SOP topology; journal version appeared in 2025.
github.com/aiwaves-cn/agents.lesson: Back-propagate textual requirements through recorded node trajectories, then optimize node definitions and graph structure through separate validated operations.

github.com/yaoz720/swarmagenticcode.name: YaoZ720/SwarmAgenticCode
github.com/yaoz720/swarmagenticcode.what: Official EMNLP 2025 implementation of from-scratch multi-agent generation using language-driven particle swarm optimization.
github.com/yaoz720/swarmagenticcode.lesson: Maintain personal-best, global-best, and failure-memory signals while mutating both agent roles and coordination topology; persist particles and traces in JSONL.

github.com/taowangcheng/sepo.name: taowangcheng/SePO
github.com/taowangcheng/sepo.what: June 2026 landing repository for self-evolving prompt-agent optimization; code release is still in preparation.
github.com/taowangcheng/sepo.lesson: Treat the improver agent's own definition as a pre-trainable optimization target, then freeze and reuse it across target-agent improvement runs.

github.com/junjie-ye/cctu.name: Junjie-Ye/CCTU
github.com/junjie-ye/cctu.what: March 2026 benchmark with 200 tool-use cases, 12 constraint categories, and executable step-level constraint checking.
github.com/junjie-ye/cctu.lesson: Compile agent-definition clauses into executable per-step invariants instead of judging only final task success.

github.com/car-bench/car-bench.name: CAR-bench/car-bench
github.com/car-bench/car-bench.what: 2026 benchmark separating base, deliberate-unsatisfiability, and disambiguation tasks in a mutable 58-tool environment governed by 19 policies.
github.com/car-bench/car-bench.lesson: Treat acting, refusing, and asking for clarification as distinct benchmark families with separate promotion gates.

github.com/claw-eval/claw-eval.name: claw-eval/claw-eval
github.com/claw-eval/claw-eval.what: March 2026 agent harness with 300 human-verified tasks, 2,159 rubrics, full-trajectory auditing, and a Pass^3 reliability metric.
github.com/claw-eval/claw-eval.lesson: Promotion should require repeated success on critical behaviors and distinguish infrastructure retries from behavioral failures.

github.com/agentevals-dev/agentevals.name: agentevals-dev/agentevals
github.com/agentevals-dev/agentevals.what: Active trace-evaluation framework with golden eval sets, deterministic or custom evaluators, CI thresholds, and a July 10 2026 release.
github.com/agentevals-dev/agentevals.lesson: Store normalized execution traces so evaluators can be independently versioned and replayed without conflating judge changes with agent executions.

github.com/harbor-framework/harbor.name: harbor-framework/harbor
github.com/harbor-framework/harbor.what: Active agent-evaluation framework; v0.18.0 released 2026-07-07, with many executor adapters and isolated verifier environments.
github.com/harbor-framework/harbor.lesson: Normalize trials across executors and verify candidates in a separately provisioned environment receiving only explicit artifacts.

github.com/sentient-agi/evoskill.name: sentient-agi/EvoSkill
github.com/sentient-agi/evoskill.what: Agent-program evolution implementation; v1.3.0 released 2026-06-16, supporting Codex, OpenCode, OpenHands, Goose, Claude Code, and Harbor.
github.com/sentient-agi/evoskill.lesson: Evolve prompts and skills as versioned packages, retain a small candidate frontier, and hide validation examples from the proposer.

github.com/maitrix-org/promptagent.name: maitrix-org/PromptAgent
github.com/maitrix-org/promptagent.what: ICLR 2024 reference implementation combining textual-gradient prompt mutation with MCTS; older but algorithmically concrete.
github.com/maitrix-org/promptagent.lesson: Use failure-derived textual gradients as mutation proposals and tree search for exploration, while replacing scalar reward with behavior-family vectors.

github.com/agiresearch/asb.name: agiresearch/ASB
github.com/agiresearch/asb.what: ICLR 2025 agent-security benchmark covering direct input, observation, planning-prompt, and memory perturbations across multiple agents and scenarios.
github.com/agiresearch/asb.lesson: Convert its attack-locus matrix into known-defect injections and measure repair success separately from retained clean-task utility.

github.com/normster/systemcheck.name: normster/SystemCheck
github.com/normster/systemcheck.what: Small 2025 system-prompt robustness implementation with utility suites, adversarial stress tests, bootstrap intervals, and 2026 evaluation additions.
github.com/normster/systemcheck.lesson: Treat instruction-pressure robustness and ordinary capability retention as matched suites with uncertainty intervals rather than one mean score.

github.com/microsoftarchive/promptbench.name: microsoftarchive/promptbench
github.com/microsoftarchive/promptbench.what: Archived but concrete prompt-robustness framework with character-, word-, sentence-, and semantic-level attacks, DyVal dynamic evaluation, and transferability analysis.
github.com/microsoftarchive/promptbench.lesson: Represent semantic-preserving perturbations as a separate evaluation axis and gate on worst-variant behavior rather than replay variance alone.

github.com/thudm/agentbench.name: THUDM/AgentBench
github.com/thudm/agentbench.what: Multi-environment executable agent benchmark; updated February 2026, with a 2025 function-calling revision and containerized task workers.
github.com/thudm/agentbench.lesson: Use independently hosted environment families as untouched-behavior sentinels so optimization on one task family cannot masquerade as general agent improvement.

github.com/stanford-crfm/helm.name: stanford-crfm/helm
github.com/stanford-crfm/helm.what: Typed holistic-evaluation framework separating scenarios, adapters, run specifications, and metrics; v0.5.15 appeared in April 2026 before maintenance mode.
github.com/stanford-crfm/helm.lesson: Version task semantics, prompt adapters, and metrics independently so evaluator changes cannot silently redefine candidate fitness.

github.com/invariantlabs-ai/invariant.name: invariantlabs-ai/invariant
github.com/invariantlabs-ai/invariant.what: Trace-policy engine for messages, tool calls, outputs, and ordered information flows; latest commit observed January 2026.
github.com/invariantlabs-ai/invariant.lesson: Compile agent clauses into executable trace invariants so promotion tests authority and action flow, not prompt wording.

github.com/langwatch/scenario.name: langwatch/scenario
github.com/langwatch/scenario.what: Python/TypeScript/Go multi-turn agent simulation framework with scripted assertions, user simulators, judges, red teaming, caching, and a July 2026 release.
github.com/langwatch/scenario.lesson: Generate positive, negative-control, conflict, and adversarial conversations from each changed behavioral family.

github.com/metr/public-tasks.name: METR/public-tasks
github.com/metr/public-tasks.what: Versioned, containerized Task Standard suite with 31 public tasks, protected evaluation guidance, and an improve_agent family; updated January 2026.
github.com/metr/public-tasks.lesson: Package agent canaries as versioned environment-plus-scorer units and explicitly track when exposure invalidates promotive evidence.

github.com/jaineet17/causal-agent-replay.name: jaineet17/causal-agent-replay
github.com/jaineet17/causal-agent-replay.what: June 2026 alpha implementation of counterfactual trajectory replay with context/policy interventions, confidence intervals, and budgeted Monte Carlo Shapley attribution.
github.com/jaineet17/causal-agent-replay.lesson: Attribute regressions to changed clauses, executors, or interactions by intervention before generating another candidate.

github.com/peiyu-georgia-li/atlas.name: Peiyu-Georgia-Li/ATLAS
github.com/peiyu-georgia-li/atlas.what: ICML 2026 Spotlight implementation of IRT-calibrated adaptive evaluation using maximum Fisher information and precision-based stopping.
github.com/peiyu-georgia-li/atlas.lesson: Allocate expensive executor-seed-perturbation cells adaptively while retaining mandatory family and executor coverage.

github.com/lmarena/p2l.name: lmarena/p2l
github.com/lmarena/p2l.what: 2025 Prompt-to-Leaderboard implementation with prompt-conditioned Bradley-Terry strengths and OpenAI-compatible cost-aware routing.
github.com/lmarena/p2l.lesson: Replace one global model rank with task-conditioned strengths, then solve routing under explicit cost constraints using the route server's BT/LP pattern.

github.com/aims-foundations/reeval.name: aims-foundations/reeval
github.com/aims-foundations/reeval.what: 2025 HELM-integrated Rasch/IRT calibration and computerized adaptive testing implementation.
github.com/aims-foundations/reeval.lesson: Estimate task difficulty and candidate ability, then spend evaluation budget on maximally informative cases while retaining explicit coverage quotas.

github.com/ibm/eval-assist.name: IBM/eval-assist
github.com/ibm/eval-assist.what: AAAI 2025 judge-development environment with direct and pairwise modes, synthetic edge cases, positional-bias inspection, and Unitxt export; v1.0.5 released November 2025.
github.com/ibm/eval-assist.lesson: Treat rubric and judge construction as a testable artifact: exercise criteria against edge cases, mirror response order, and preserve the resulting judge configuration for bulk replay.

github.com/code2bench/code2bench.name: Code2Bench/Code2Bench
github.com/code2bench/code2bench.what: ICLR 2026 dynamic code-benchmark compiler using post-cutoff commits, scope graphs, property-based tests, full branch-coverage filtering, and failure fingerprints.
github.com/code2bench/code2bench.lesson: Compile fresh task-local cases only when generated oracles pass strong structural and behavioral validation; emit diagnostic failure classes rather than only Pass@1.

github.com/megagonlabs/funcbenchgen.name: megagonlabs/FuncBenchGen
github.com/megagonlabs/funcbenchgen.what: ICLR 2026 generator for contamination-free multi-step tool tasks over hidden dependency DAGs with controllable depth, width, and type-compatible distractors.
github.com/megagonlabs/funcbenchgen.lesson: Generate sealed counterfactual tool-use cases from hidden graphs and vary one difficulty factor at a time to localize model versus state-tracking failures.

github.com/openai/evals.name: openai/evals
github.com/openai/evals.what: Custom and private eval framework with registry data, templates, completion-function protocols, and approximately 691 commits; verified July 2026.
github.com/openai/evals.lesson: Make private workflow data a first-class evaluation source while retaining reusable scorer and dispatcher interfaces.

github.com/livecodebench/livecodebench.name: LiveCodeBench/LiveCodeBench
github.com/livecodebench/livecodebench.what: Continuously versioned coding benchmark with time-window filtering in lcb_runner/evaluation/compute_scores.py and custom-output evaluation; release_v6 covers problems through April 2025.
github.com/livecodebench/livecodebench.lesson: Attach source dates and evaluation windows to private cases, rotate them, and compare rankings on post-release cases to expose contamination-sensitive reversals.

github.com/vllm-project/guidellm.name: vllm-project/guidellm
github.com/vllm-project/guidellm.what: Production-workload benchmark supporting file and trace inputs, traffic profiles, token-level and tail-latency metrics; v0.7.1 released July 2, 2026.
github.com/vllm-project/guidellm.lesson: Replay the real workload shape and carry TTFT, inter-token latency, end-to-end latency, throughput, and SLO violations beside quality.

github.com/llwork8888/creditaudit.name: LLwork8888/CreditAudit
github.com/llwork8888/creditaudit.what: Small 2026 toolkit whose creditaudit_test.py evaluates models under aligned benign system-prompt scenarios and whose draw_a_picture.py reports mean and protocol-induced volatility.
github.com/llwork8888/creditaudit.lesson: Benchmark semantically invariant protocol siblings and treat dispersion or worst-slice loss as deployment risk, not merely noise around a mean.

github.com/ulab-uiuc/llmrouter.name: ulab-uiuc/LLMRouter
github.com/ulab-uiuc/llmrouter.what: Actively updated routing library with 16-plus routers, custom_tasks, price metadata, token-bearing routing records, and a July 2026 update.
github.com/ulab-uiuc/llmrouter.lesson: Represent router replay and live execution as comparable policies over the same private task rows, including token use, per-model prices, and route regret.

github.com/ianarawjo/evalstats.name: ianarawjo/evalstats
github.com/ianarawjo/evalstats.what: Paired and nested-bootstrap LLM evaluation statistics with PPI correction; PyPI v0.1.9 released April 2026.
github.com/ianarawjo/evalstats.lesson: Use item-paired nested inference, multiplicity correction, rank probabilities, and sparse-oracle prediction-powered correction instead of aggregate means.

github.com/dustalov/evalica.name: dustalov/evalica
github.com/dustalov/evalica.what: Rust/Python pairwise-ranking toolkit implementing Elo, Bradley-Terry, bootstrap uncertainty, and Krippendorff alpha; v0.4.2 released April 2026.
github.com/dustalov/evalica.lesson: Provide fast descriptive pairwise ranks and reliability estimates, while reserving promotion authority for uncertainty-aware and bias-adjusted models.

github.com/huggingface/datatrove.name: huggingface/datatrove
github.com/huggingface/datatrove.what: Large-scale data processing and decontamination library; v0.9.0 released March 2026.
github.com/huggingface/datatrove.lesson: Build task-specific n-gram indexes and retain the matched n-gram and source task as item-level provenance rather than returning only a contamination flag.

github.com/allenai/fluid-benchmarking.name: allenai/fluid-benchmarking
github.com/allenai/fluid-benchmarking.what: COLM 2025 implementation of 2PL IRT fitting, MAP ability estimation, and Fisher-information-guided adaptive benchmark selection.
github.com/allenai/fluid-benchmarking.lesson: Estimate item difficulty and discrimination, demote saturated items, and allocate diagnostic trials adaptively while preserving common confirmatory anchors.

github.com/sksoumik/llm-as-judge.name: sksoumik/llm-as-judge
github.com/sksoumik/llm-as-judge.what: April 2026 preprint implementation comparing nine judge-debiasing strategies with controlled position, length, style, and model-origin pairs; four commits and no release.
github.com/sksoumik/llm-as-judge.lesson: Measure each judge's actual bias profile and choose mitigation per judge and task cluster instead of applying a fixed swap or ensemble recipe.

github.com/eth-sri/constat.name: eth-sri/ConStat
github.com/eth-sri/constat.what: Performance-based contamination test with bootstrap effect estimates; last repository commit visible July 2024.
github.com/eth-sri/constat.lesson: Compare anchor and fresh reference-suite performance through a reference-model difficulty map, then gate on estimated inflation and decision sensitivity rather than lexical overlap alone.

github.com/sambowyer/bayes_evals.name: sambowyer/bayes_evals
github.com/sambowyer/bayes_evals.what: Lightweight paired Bayesian analysis for binary LLM evaluations; accompanies an ICML 2025 Spotlight position paper and has 26 commits.
github.com/sambowyer/bayes_evals.lesson: For small binary gold sets, report posterior pairwise superiority and intervals instead of treating three repetitions or asymptotic error bars as decisive.

github.com/penfever/judgment-to-noise.name: penfever/judgment-to-noise
github.com/penfever/judgment-to-noise.what: September 2025 rubric-validity toolkit implementing schematic adherence, factor analysis, reliability, HTMT, cross-loading, and uncertainty-aware reporting.
github.com/penfever/judgment-to-noise.lesson: Self-audit whether judge verdicts follow declared rubric factors and whether supposedly distinct dimensions have collapsed before allowing their aggregate to select a model.

github.com/skylenage-ai/hle-verified.name: SKYLENAGE-AI/HLE-Verified
github.com/skylenage-ai/hle-verified.what: February 2026 structured audit and repair of 2,500 HLE items with component verification, uncertainty quarantine, and 150 repository commits.
github.com/skylenage-ai/hle-verified.lesson: Version problem, answer, and rationale validity separately; use independent repair and adjudication, retain uncertain items diagnostically, and exclude them from promotion evidence.

github.com/shreyaskc/babeljudge.name: Shreyaskc/BabelJudge
github.com/shreyaskc/babeljudge.what: June 2026 judge-audit framework with 31 commits, controlled degradations, two-order evaluation, and text plus agent-trajectory perturbations.
github.com/shreyaskc/babeljudge.lesson: Generate local metamorphic judge probes for position, verbosity, missing steps, corrupted arguments, and hallucinated tools, then emit a judge reliability card separate from candidate scores.

github.com/ibm/unitxt.name: IBM/unitxt
github.com/ibm/unitxt.what: Composable evaluation recipes built from task definitions, templates, preprocessors, metrics, formats, and model-agnostic inference; latest observed release 1.26.10 on 2026-05-27.
github.com/ibm/unitxt.lesson: Represent each local benchmark as an executable typed recipe rather than an informal prompt-and-fixture bundle, then hash the resolved recipe into the trial identity.

github.com/shreyaskc/evalci.name: Shreyaskc/evalci
github.com/shreyaskc/evalci.what: New July 2026 library exposing paired permutation and McNemar tests, bootstrap intervals, clustered intervals, power analysis, Holm correction, and eval-harness adapters.
github.com/shreyaskc/evalci.lesson: Turn per-item trial rows into a statistical selection certificate containing delta, confidence interval, power or minimum detectable effect, and multiplicity-adjusted evidence.

github.com/mjpost/sacrebleu.name: mjpost/sacrebleu
github.com/mjpost/sacrebleu.what: Reference evaluation implementation whose output embeds metric configuration in a compact signature; release v2.6.0 observed on 2026-01-12.
github.com/mjpost/sacrebleu.lesson: Attach a canonical benchmark signature to every score so fixture, preprocessing, metric, judge, and version differences cannot masquerade as model differences.

github.com/llm-d/llm-d-benchmark.name: llm-d/llm-d-benchmark
github.com/llm-d/llm-d-benchmark.what: Declarative inference benchmarking with rendered configuration, swappable harnesses, workload profiles, deployment validation, and factorial experiment treatments; v0.7.0 observed on 2026-06-25.
github.com/llm-d/llm-d-benchmark.lesson: Benchmark candidates under observed traffic envelopes, using workload profiles for request rate, concurrency, context length, output length, prefix reuse, and multi-turn behavior rather than measuring isolated calls.

github.com/benchflow-ai/skillsbench.name: benchflow-ai/skillsbench
github.com/benchflow-ai/skillsbench.what: 2026 benchmark using matched no-skill, curated-skill, and self-generated-skill conditions across model-harness configurations.
github.com/benchflow-ai/skillsbench.lesson: Treat skill availability as an intervention and report paired skill lift separately from invocation rate and absolute executor success.

github.com/langchain-ai/skills-benchmark.name: langchain-ai/skills-benchmarks
github.com/langchain-ai/skills-benchmark.what: Treatment-oriented skill benchmark with control, production, split/merged, concise, and noise-skill conditions; v0.1.0 released March 2026.
github.com/langchain-ai/skills-benchmark.lesson: Decouple task definitions from skill treatments and add distractor libraries to measure skill selection and interference, not only assisted task completion.

github.com/geniushtx/swe-skills-bench.name: GeniusHTX/SWE-Skills-Bench
github.com/geniushtx/swe-skills-bench.what: March 2026 requirement-driven paired evaluation of public SWE skills against fixed-commit repositories and deterministic tests.
github.com/geniushtx/swe-skills-bench.lesson: Bind every gold criterion to an executable check and test version compatibility because stale skill guidance can create negative lift.

github.com/cxcscmu/skilllearnbench.name: cxcscmu/SkillLearnBench
github.com/cxcscmu/skilllearnbench.what: COLM 2026 benchmark evaluating generated skills at content, execution-trajectory, and task-outcome levels.
github.com/cxcscmu/skilllearnbench.lesson: Use layered mediation metrics to distinguish defective skill content from executor non-adoption and downstream execution failure.

github.com/salesforceairesearch/mcp-univ.name: SalesforceAIResearch/MCP-Universe
github.com/salesforceairesearch/mcp-univ.what: 2025 real-world MCP benchmark framework with composable evaluators, traces, live environments, and v1.1.3 released March 2026.
github.com/salesforceairesearch/mcp-univ.lesson: Support evaluator pipelines expressed as extraction functions plus operators, including executable dynamic ground truth for time-sensitive skill tasks.

github.com/ucsb-nlp-chang/skill-usage.name: UCSB-NLP-Chang/Skill-Usage
github.com/ucsb-nlp-chang/skill-usage.what: April 2026 skill-utility study with retrieval from 34k skills, three models, trajectory inspection, and refinement pipelines.
github.com/ucsb-nlp-chang/skill-usage.lesson: Separate oracle content efficacy from retrieval, selection, and realized end-to-end skill lift.

github.com/agent-ecosystem/agent-skill-i.name: agent-ecosystem/agent-skill-implementation
github.com/agent-ecosystem/agent-skill-i.what: Nascent 2026 compatibility project with 17 canary-bearing skills covering 23 loader checks across nine categories.
github.com/agent-ecosystem/agent-skill-i.lesson: Use resource-specific canaries to distinguish harness exposure from model activation and instruction-following.

github.com/anthropics/skills.name: anthropics/skills
github.com/anthropics/skills.what: Current official Agent Skills repository; its skill-creator snapshots old/no-skill baselines and grades repeated outputs.
github.com/anthropics/skills.lesson: Treat the skill directory as the versioned intervention and preserve immutable baseline snapshots per evaluation.

github.com/agentskills/agentskills.name: agentskills/agentskills
github.com/agentskills/agentskills.what: Active open specification repository defining portable bundles and progressive discovery, activation, and resource loading.
github.com/agentskills/agentskills.lesson: Benchmark each progressive-disclosure stage independently; format conformance does not prove runtime-semantic portability.

github.com/py-why/econml.name: py-why/EconML
github.com/py-why/econml.what: Heterogeneous-treatment-effect toolkit; latest verified release v0.16.0 was July 2025.
github.com/py-why/econml.lesson: Estimate skill uplift conditional on executor and task features, with intervals, once the factorial run matrix is large enough.

github.com/nvidia/skills.name: NVIDIA/skills
github.com/nvidia/skills.what: Daily-synced signed skill catalog with evaluation datasets, skill cards, and per-skill benchmark reports; active through June/July 2026.
github.com/nvidia/skills.lesson: Co-locate provenance and uplift evidence, but generate all views from one content-addressed effect certificate to prevent freshness divergence.

github.com/ynulihao/agentskillos.name: ynulihao/AgentSkillOS
github.com/ynulihao/agentskillos.what: Hierarchical skill retrieval and DAG orchestration over large registries, with benchmark and modular architecture released in March 2026.
github.com/ynulihao/agentskillos.lesson: Turn registry size and distractor similarity into controlled benchmark factors and instrument retrieval separately from orchestration.

github.com/saint-viperx/scr_bench.name: saint-viperx/SCR_Bench
github.com/saint-viperx/scr_bench.what: June 2026 skill-composition security benchmark covering capability flow, trust transfer, and authorization confusion.
github.com/saint-viperx/scr_bench.lesson: Evaluate activated skill paths with isolated and composed treatment arms; benign per-skill scores do not imply benign composition.

github.com/salib/salib.name: SALib/SALib
github.com/salib/salib.what: Active Python sensitivity-analysis library with fractional-factorial, Morris, Sobol, and interaction analysis; v1.5.2 released October 2025.
github.com/salib/salib.lesson: Use fractional-factorial screening and dummy factors to find influential bundle components before paying for confirmatory interaction trials.

github.com/shap/shap.name: shap/shap
github.com/shap/shap.what: Game-theoretic attribution library with exact, partition/Owen, and pairwise interaction mechanisms; v0.52.0 released May 28, 2026.
github.com/shap/shap.lesson: After screening reduces the coalition space, attribute bundle value and interactions while respecting hierarchical component groupings.

insights.insight: A research plan should be a versioned executable object: question dependencies, required evidence classes, counter-searches, and approval state must survive execution.
Research telemetry becomes actionable when costs are attributed to queries, citations, and supported claims rather than only to iterations or tokens.
The continuation primitive should be structured as gap plus originating evidence plus follow-up query plus expected closure condition.
Maintaining and auditing a live draft during retrieval converts synthesis defects into targeted research actions and reduces information loss between investigation and final writing.
The defensible state unit is not an iteration summary but a versioned claim-evidence decision: query recipe, source version, selector, support relation, inclusion decision, and supersession history.
A living-research resume should rerun frozen search recipes, diff result IDs and content digests, screen only deltas, and propagate source changes through claim dependencies.

Question selection can be treated as active acquisition: learn expected evidence yield from prior branch outcomes and combine it with contradiction risk, source-independence gain, staleness, impact, and cost.

Current deep-research benchmarks primarily grade terminal report quality; replay equivalence, updateability, evidence lineage, and change detection remain largely unmeasured and therefore form a credible differentiation axis.

Quality guards that run only on STOP are too late: an invalid iteration can already poison strategy, findings, and progressive synthesis. Add a pre-reducer admission verdict that can preserve raw evidence while withholding it from trusted state.

Citation reliability has two independent layers: source identity and claim entailment. A real paper with mismatched metadata fails identity; a correctly identified paper that does not support the claim fails entailment.

Raw novelty is not a safe convergence variable because fabrication, duplicated syndication, and poisoned content can all appear novel. Only admitted, independently sourced claim deltas should contribute to evidence yield.

Confirmation bias needs a claim-level falsification receipt, not generic source diversity. DeepTRACE found one-sided outputs even when deep-research configurations achieved high citation thoroughness.

Reproducible failure evaluation needs controlled evidence, distractors, noise, and poisoned variants. Static sandboxes make false admission, missed evidence, and guard false positives measurable across runtime versions.
The synthesis artifact should be a materialized view of a versioned atomic-claim ledger, with evidence-span and derivation edges, rather than the primary mutable state.
Support retrieval, contradiction retrieval, and answer generation should be separate operations so a fluent synthesis cannot conceal missing or conflicting evidence.
Claim evaluation should allow multiple valid evidence passages and score grounded abstention, not require one canonical citation or reward unsupported completeness.
Retraction and update metadata are useful change signals but unsafe as automatic truth; they should reopen dependent claims for verification.
A review pass should emit candidates, not verdict-bearing findings; semantic clustering, claim-level replication, and a separately prompted validator should precede P0/P1 activation.

Impact, confidence, actionability, evidence type, and reachability are orthogonal. Encoding all of them in P0/P1/P2 makes low-confidence catastrophic hypotheses indistinguishable from reproduced blockers.

False-positive learning should produce scoped, inspectable suppression records with rationale, applicability conditions, expiry or invalidation triggers, and shadow-audit results—not opaque prompt memory.

The meaningful optimization target is a precision-recall frontier plus downstream resolution and rebuttal rates, stratified by category and severity; novelty convergence alone cannot detect a confidently noisy reviewer.
Multi-review diversity should be treated as a recall stage, not a truth vote: aggregate candidates first, then require independent validation before publishing them.
insights.evidence: SWR-Bench reports up to 43.67% F1 improvement from multi-review aggregation on 1,000 verified PRs: https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.01494

insights.insight: The useful unit of adversarial review is a falsifiable execution or data-flow claim, with intermediate facts retained so a validator can attack the weakest link.
Iterative verification should narrow around disputed findings and uncovered dimensions; whole-project repetition has weaker and less consistent returns.
insights.evidence: Detect--Repair--Verify found bounded iteration better than single-pass but with clearer gains at narrower scopes: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.23633

insights.insight: Changed-line mutation testing can measure whether tests are sensitive to the reviewed behavior without paying whole-repository mutation cost.
insights.evidence: Google incremental code-review mutation study and Stryker incremental line scoping: https://research.google/pubs/practical-mutation-testing-at-scale-a-view-from-google/ ; https://stryker-mutator.io/docs/stryker-js/incremental/

insights.insight: The judge should see only the semantic residual left after schema, build, test, diff-filtered static analysis, and claim-specific executable checks; deterministic failures and eliminations remain first-class receipts.
Refute-by-N must count independent evidence classes rather than repeated LLM votes; require a two-sided executable control so a malformed generated reproducer cannot confirm its own finding.

Cross-pass finding identity should use versioned partial fingerprints based on symbols, normalized source context, program slices, and rename mapping; baseline state should distinguish new, updated, unchanged, and absent.

P0/P1/P2 should remain an impact axis while confidence, reachability, exploitability, evidence strength, and evidence scope are recorded independently; otherwise repeated judge agreement can incorrectly inflate severity.
A blocking finding needs two independent axes: impact and evidential confidence. High impact alone is a verification priority, not proof.
The proposed fix is a falsifiable counterfactual: execute original and revised implementations against existing and spec-augmented tests, and classify non-improving fixes as overcorrection evidence.
Reviewer versions need shadow probation on known-clean and known-defective controls; pass health should gate whether any newly generated finding is admitted.
Coverage should be measured across reasoning distance, while context is escalated per cell. Flat full-context prompts can reduce detection even when token growth is small.
Sycophancy is detectable as evidence-invariant verdict instability: paired neutral and author-opinion renderings should produce identical severity unless new evidence is introduced.
Construct a semantic residual by subtracting syntax-equivalent and explicitly modeled semantics-preserving edits before allocating LLM review passes.
The natural path-coverage cell for deep-review is changed semantic node to affected observable boundary, represented as a program slice with explicit unmodeled external calls.
Finding continuity should use introduced/fixed/preexisting lineage plus stable semantic anchors, allowing deep-review to distinguish a resolved defect from one moved or re-expressed by a later patch.
A runtime difference is a witness, not a verdict: it must pass stability, causal-proximity, and relevance checks before changing finding confidence.

Selective semantic-equivalence checking is best used as the expensive terminal verifier for scoped high-risk functions; inability to prove equivalence must remain distinct from a demonstrated behavior change.
Compile each council into shared common ground plus disjoint private evidence; exchange rationale excerpts so private signals can cross seat boundaries without collapsing the information partition.
Generate task-specific independent reasoning paths before fan-out, use unique paths for exploration and duplicate paths only when consistency checking is the better objective.
Construct recipient-specific inboxes from original dissenting message IDs; disagreement preservation is safer and more informative than confidence-threshold filtering or transcript summarization.
Track probabilistic beliefs, calibration, revisions, and oscillations as first-class council state; textual agreement alone cannot distinguish correct convergence from herding.

Constitutional deliberation needs hard veto constraints, soft multi-objective scoring, and an explicit minority artifact because maximum consensus or cooperation can reduce autonomy, integrity, or fairness.

Debate communication has no inherent positive drift: without a mechanism favoring independently supported correct signals, its expected gain can collapse to ordinary voting or stochastic re-evaluation.
Effective seat count should be estimated from historical error correlation; architectural heterogeneity changes the scaling regime, while extra dense communication can destroy answer diversity.
Reasoning-method diversity is more load-bearing than persona diversity: seats need different algorithms or evidence partitions, not merely Analyst, Critic, and Pragmatist labels.

Identity cues create measurable sycophancy and self-bias that accumulate across rounds; anonymized claim exchange and counterbalanced adjudication convert authority-sensitive debate into content-sensitive evaluation.

Minority disagreement is an information channel, not convergence noise: debate dynamics can identify when a correlated majority suppressed the correct alternative, but an LLM judge may share the majority's blind spot.

Categorical council votes should be aggregated with task-conditioned seat confusion matrices and hierarchical priors; provider and prompt lineage must be modeled as correlated independence clusters or the posterior will overcount cloned errors.
Blinded A/B and B/A judgments can be folded into a Bradley-Terry posterior with covariance; order disagreement is a judge defect or escalation signal, not an additional vote.
