Debate has its strongest empirical case under information asymmetry and unreliable consultancy; against direct answering without that asymmetry its benefit is mixed, so protocol routing should test for evidence asymmetry before paying for debate.
Seat dispatch can be formulated as Bayesian experimental design over seat, item, and protocol, optimizing expected entropy reduction per token instead of filling a fixed council roster.

Selective judge adaptation should trigger on swap inconsistency, but its evolving prompt is order-sensitive; calibration state must therefore be learned on a separate stream and frozen with a packet-visible version hash.
A stance flip is evidence of learning only when it can be linked to newly introduced, independently verified claim IDs; majority-aligned flips without evidence gain are a direct conformity signal.

Falling disagreement is not inherently progress: track dispersion contraction jointly with verified-evidence gain and minority-claim disposition, because discussion can compress ratings and propagate biased opinions.

Majority size, perceived expertise, argument length, and rhetorical style are treatment variables; anonymization, length normalization, and order permutation can therefore serve as causal stability probes before convergence.
Council synthesis should operate on atomic claims and exact leave-one-seat-out sensitivity, producing supported consensus, contested claims, and unresolved minorities rather than a scalar winner.
Council value is sharply task-conditional: hidden-profile and consequential process-accountability tasks are the plausible moat, while routine tasks should route to a strong single model.
Private pre-discussion estimates, evidence-focused discussion, and private post-discussion re-estimation separate information transfer from conformity and make influence measurable.

A council verdict should preserve a pairwise option graph with ties and cycles; a Condorcet winner is useful when it exists, while a cycle is information about unresolved trade-offs rather than an aggregation failure.
Counterfactual fork-and-diff turns susceptibility to speaker identity, order, verbosity, claimed expertise, and majority cues into a deterministic council quality test.

Treat evaluator traces, error messages, profiler output, and constraint failures as an actionable textual gradient; scalar dimension scores remain promotion constraints but are insufficient mutation guidance.

Maintain two selection structures: a case-wise Pareto frontier for complementary competence and a MAP-Elites archive over stable behavioral descriptors; do not use the five score dimensions themselves as permissive trade-off axes.

Mutation operators can themselves be candidates: record operator lineage and evolve diagnostic, crossover, and architecture-synthesis policies, but keep evaluator implementation and promotion thresholds outside the mutable genome.
A frozen LLM judge can still be gamed if it sees the optimized candidate before forming an independent answer; promotion should include a candidate-blind commitment or verifiable hidden anchor.
Under heterogeneous or drifting workloads, improvement should produce a routed tree of profile-specific champions; global replacement is reserved for candidates that transfer across profiles.
A frozen evaluator is an experiment-scoped commitment, not a permanently trustworthy objective: re-baseline both candidate and baseline whenever an evaluator epoch changes.
Evaluator secrecy and query control are separate from evaluator immutability; repeated scalar feedback can reveal exploitable judge preferences without any evaluator write access.
Require conservative cross-family judge agreement plus deterministic anchors; diverse reward-model ensembles using worst-case or uncertainty-weighted objectives reduce proxy overoptimization.
The evaluator must be outside the mutable genome at the capability level, because models trained on specification gaming have generalized to modifying rewards and tests directly.

Promotion is a deployment experiment rather than a file-copy decision: shadow evidence and bounded canary exposure measure behavior on the operating distribution while preserving a stable rollback target.

Raw trial evidence must be append-only and reduction-independent: candidate, evaluator, fixture, seed, judge family, raw scale, rationale digest, normalization version, cost, and latency should survive every later score-policy change.
Candidate-blind judging should run both presentation orders; disagreement between AB and BA is evidence of evaluator instability, not noise to average away silently.
Evolutionary mutation is useful for breadth, but survival should use per-dimension Pareto or lexicase selection so gains in one dimension cannot conceal regressions in another.
Metamorphic evaluation converts underspecified robustness expectations into executable paired oracles and can detect regressions without requiring a unique gold output.

Classify every promotion failure as regressional, extremal, causal, or adversarial Goodhart and attach a distinct detection receipt and veto; generic benchmark_failed loses the evidence needed to improve the evaluator.
Evaluator success and evaluator-integrity oversight must be separate outputs: a candidate can pass the nominal task while modifying reward, tests, caches, or evidence.
Adaptive evaluator feedback is an information leak; reveal only thresholded verdict bands during optimization and withhold exact scores and fixture identities until session termination.
Canary freshness needs an explicit lifecycle—sealed, active, burned, retired—and semantic leakage detection because paraphrases and translations bypass literal matching.

Optimization pressure is itself a risk signal: track cumulative evaluator queries and candidate fan-out against sealed/gold gain, and stop when proxy gain continues while independent gain stalls or reverses.
Lane A currently generates separate candidate and baseline profiles, so the measured delta compares two subject-derived check sets; freezing weights alone does not freeze the evaluator.

insights.insight: Evaluator identity should be a versioned dependency closure over the canonical profile, rubric, fixture commitments, grader, calibration, and environment; amendments open a new epoch rather than silently changing the ruler.

Metamorphic evaluation becomes substantially more actionable when relation families are generated and failing variants are shrunk into minimal counterexamples, rather than retained as an ad hoc list of perturbations.
Real-versus-test-cued twin fixtures detect deployment/evaluation divergence that ordinary held-out accuracy misses; this should be a hard parity gate for affected agent behaviors.

Prompt improvement needs representation regularization: recurrent, broadly supported rules should survive, while batch-specific patches and style-only rewrites should be filtered before expensive evaluation.
Authority validity must be a prerequisite to artifact conformance: parse, type, capability, rule-test, coverage, and signature failures should produce AUTHORITY_INVALID rather than artifact PASS.
A typed rule IR should distinguish deterministic, schema, relational, and reasoning-required obligations so rule coverage cannot be confused with artifact coverage.
Known deviations should be first-class, scope-predicated exception records whose use remains visible as SKIP or EXEMPT, not silent suppression.

Conformance is direction-sensitive across authority epochs: replaying witnesses from the old authority against the new one detects deleted or weakened obligations that a current-snapshot audit misses.
Generated positive, negative, boundary, and stateful witnesses plus shrinking turn a semantic finding into a minimal replayable proof and reveal unexercised rule branches.

A proof-carrying finding should reference an immutable authority capsule, an explicit applicability decision, one or more subject-bound observations, and the evidence-backed verifier decision; otherwise it remains a candidate or inconclusive result.
Applicability must precede verification. Lane workers should resolve target facts against authority-specific predicates and return not_applicable or unresolved before running expensive judges.

Known deviations are safer as chronological assertions than filters: retain the original failure, record the accepted disposition, continue verification, and invalidate the assertion when authority, subject, verifier, scope, or time changes.

False-positive control should measure effective usefulness, not technical correctness alone. Promote an authority/verifier pair only after shadow sampling shows its upper confidence bound meets the lane's usefulness threshold.

Authority compilation should preserve source anchors, identifiers, profiles, applicability, and executable checks as separable layers; mutation tests can then detect deleted, weakened, inverted, narrowed, or exception-wrapped normative claims.

Authority compilation needs a governed intermediate representation: LLM extraction may propose rule trees, but deterministic validation, witnesses, source spans, and authorized publication must gate execution.
Residual executable expressions provide a concrete absence-proof mechanism: unresolved required observations can remain explicit and yield INCONCLUSIVE instead of false conformity.
Suppression is a new adjudication fact, not a destructive rewrite of the original finding; authority or verifier changes can then reactivate it deterministically.
Cross-epoch conformance requires compatibility classes rather than one global pass/fail bit because the same change may preserve one contract while breaking another.
Evidence receipts should be content-bound and freshness-aware so resume can distinguish verified observations from stale, missing, or unverifiable claims.
A readable live authority file is not necessarily current or authorized; conformance requires rollback, expiry, and mix-and-match checks over a pinned authority epoch.
Suppression state can silently preserve obsolete verifier capabilities, so every deviation must bind to both an authority epoch and verifier digest.
Completeness must be measured against declared applicability edges, not only discovered artifacts; otherwise a broken discovery adapter reports perfect coverage.
Precision and recall can coexist through asymmetric verdict authority: high-precision rules may block, while extended rules create candidates requiring independent confirmation.

An alignment certificate needs separate fields for requirement, claim, counterclaim, supporting evidence, counter-evidence, assessor, conformance, and confidence; combining these into one finding confidence loses the distinction between truth, evidence strength, and evaluator certainty.

Replay must address subjects and verifier components by digest. Re-evaluating a mutable path or tag can validate a different object than the original audit and creates a time-of-check/time-of-use gap.

Structured assurance permits incremental semantic regression: propagate typed changes forward through evidence and backward through claims, preserving unaffected conclusions and marking only affected conclusions for replay or review.

Alignment outcomes must distinguish failure from cannot-determine, inapplicability, and untested scope, and must record whether the judgment was automatic, semi-automatic, or manual; otherwise unknown coverage silently becomes PASS.
Replace monolithic prompt mutation with a typed AgentIR covering role, instructions, tools, routing, memory, verifier policy, and inference configuration, with explicit mutable and immutable loci.

Turn every failing behavior benchmark into component-specific actionable side information by locating the first divergent trace event and propagating requirements backward only to implicated components.
Keep candidate lineages on per-case and per-objective Pareto frontiers, and merge only compatible component-level strengths rather than selecting or splicing whole prompts by aggregate score.
Pre-train the improver agent across heterogeneous historical failures, but freeze it during target optimization and keep evaluation and promotion outside the self-referential loop.
Promote a conditional operational envelope rather than a universal prompt because prompt structure, decoding configuration, model version, and task distribution interact.

Behavior coverage should be computed over agent-definition clauses, authority-conflict pairs, state transitions, and environmental perturbations; task-count coverage alone cannot show that the agent's discipline contract was exercised.

Negative capability is a first-class agent behavior: deliberate impossibility and ambiguity cases must test whether the candidate refuses, defers, or clarifies instead of maximizing nominal completion.
A reusable normalized trajectory separates execution evidence from evaluator versions, allowing cheap evaluator replay while preserving the requirement to freshly execute every changed candidate.
Reliable promotion needs two statistical regimes: zero-tolerance repeated passes for critical invariants, and paired family-level confidence bounds for broad task suites.

Structural error analysis plus automatic rollback and a guard-metric floor can make prompt optimization monotone on measured objectives, but agent definitions additionally require authority, side-effect, and inheritance invariants.
Known-defect injection at a declared prompt, tool, routing, memory, or executor locus makes causal-blame accuracy measurable instead of inferred from aggregate improvement.
The useful optimizer is nested: reflective failure analysis proposes atomic diffs, while frontier search preserves behaviorally distinct candidates until ablation establishes causality.
Executor portability is an experimental axis: identical task artifacts and verifier semantics must be replayed through versioned executor adapters before promotion.
Verifier isolation belongs in the behavioral contract; changing the verifier, adapter, tool schema, or model invalidates prior promotion evidence.
Agent robustness should remain a family-level Pareto vector with confidence intervals because defensive gains can conceal clean-task capability loss.
Identical-replay stability does not detect prompt brittleness; promotion needs semantic-equivalence sets and a worst-variant or lower-tail gate.
Cross-specification and worst-family retention are necessary because aggregate task or rubric scores can conceal severe rare-family regressions.
Agent optimization must hold out environments and interaction patterns, not merely examples; held-in success can reflect memorized observation-action relations rather than adaptation.
Missing dimension samples currently masquerade as perfect stability, so the evidence gate can confuse absence of measurement with repeatability.
An agent-definition diff should compile into clause-level positive obligations, preserved-behavior controls, authority-conflict tests, perturbation families, and trace assertions.

Behavioral semantic versioning can be operational: patch means equivalence across all families, minor means localized gain plus non-inferiority elsewhere, and major means authority or capability change requiring full requalification.
insights.evidence: Synthesis from executable trace policies and scenario-based regression testing: https://github.com/invariantlabs-ai/invariant

insights.insight: Adaptive item selection should optimize information about the paired candidate-baseline delta, subject to minimum family coverage and exposure caps, rather than optimize absolute candidate ability.
Failure localization should use clause/context and executor/policy interventions with repeated forward replay; visual trace inspection or judge attribution alone is not causal evidence.
Router-A versus live-B is a model comparison only if model and execution path are independently crossed; otherwise the valid estimand is the difference between two complete candidate stacks.

Adaptive evaluation should select cases by expected information gain after minimum family coverage, using a Rasch-style ability/difficulty estimate rather than spending the same number of trials on obvious wins and ambiguous boundaries.
Judge calibration must be candidate-specific: use a small oracle slice to correct paired judge deltas and propagate calibration uncertainty; shared calibration can produce a confident sign reversal.

Contamination resistance is a case-lineage state machine: temporal source evidence, proposer visibility, oracle visibility, first exposure, disclosure, retirement, and replacement lineage must be recorded separately.

Public rankings should be treated as candidate-selection priors, because their top model and ordering often fail to transfer to unseen target tasks; report private-task rank correlation and public-top-1 regret explicitly.

The outcome metric is part of the model-selection treatment: compliance checks, numeric equivalence, executable assertions, and task-specific ground truth can reorder models relative to generic lexical or aggregate metrics.
Protocol robustness must be crossed with model and task rather than averaged away; benign formatting and system-prompt variations can move rankings or overturn deployment priorities.
Contamination resistance needs provenance, time separation, secrecy, rotation, and retirement lineage; literal duplicate detection alone cannot detect training on the broader test task.

Cost-aware selection should optimize a quality-constrained Pareto frontier using complete realized cost and latency, then allocate further samples by expected decision value rather than applying a fixed weighted score.
A benchmark needs two item pools: common sealed anchors for paired promotion inference and an adaptive diagnostic tail selected by item information under explicit family-coverage quotas.
The correct resampling unit is the task or item family, with seeds and perturbations nested beneath it; flattening fixture-sample rows understates uncertainty when variants share semantics.
Judge outputs should be treated as noisy predictions corrected by sparse randomized oracle labels per dimension and task cluster, not as ground truth validated only by global agreement.

Contamination is a versioned item-level evidence record containing detector, matched span or hash, source scope, visibility, and lineage; exact n-grams are a cheap first detector but cannot establish paraphrase cleanliness.

Pairwise rank and operational value are different estimands: fit Bradley-Terry or Davidson for preference probabilities, then apply hard quality floors and uncertain business utility to choose a deployment policy.

The shipped two-grader mechanism is not on the scoring path, and its two calls share one model family; reviewer independence must be represented by model-build provenance and enforced by dispatch, not inferred from different role prompts.

A multi-axis rubric is valid only if isolated axis perturbations move the intended subscore, leave unrelated subscores stable, and the reducer-owned total explains the final verdict; factor collapse or low schematic adherence makes the aggregate non-actionable.

Contamination should be measured as non-generalizing performance inflation relative to a fresh same-task suite and a reference-model difficulty curve, with promotion based on whether the inflation interval or exclusions change the local winner.

Sample adequacy is a property of the intended claim and independent item families, not a universal repetitions-per-cell constant; three samples can establish neither useful power nor task-distribution coverage.

Gold truth needs component-level provenance, independent reviews, final adjudication, an explicit uncertain state, and selective replay when an oracle changes; model consensus is diagnostic evidence rather than authority.

The strongest mode-specific experimental unit is a frozen workflow prefix plus environment snapshot: replay the same state while intervening only on model identity, then compare resulting actions and state transitions.

A benchmark winner is not decision-grade unless the paired design has enough resolution for the observed gap; repeated decoding must be clustered by underlying task and comparisons across candidates corrected for multiplicity.

Cost-aware selection should price task errors, latency, abstention, and switching overhead in the user's operational units; token price alone can favor a cheap model whose mistakes dominate total economic loss.

Benchmark identity must include both the semantic evaluation recipe and the deployment workload profile; identical quality fixtures can produce different valid selections under different context-length, concurrency, and tail-latency regimes.
Skill lift must be a within-task, within-executor paired contrast; an absolute with-skill score mostly measures the underlying executor and task difficulty.

Availability, invocation, trajectory compliance, and outcome are separate causal stages; primary ranking should use intention-to-treat lift while the later stages diagnose why lift was gained or lost.
Near-neighbor and noise skills are necessary negative controls because a preselected-skill benchmark does not measure discovery, routing, or library interference.
A skill score without environment and dependency compatibility metadata is unsafe: version-mismatched procedural guidance can measurably reduce success.

Gold scoring should combine deterministic final-state checks, non-binding milestone-DAG diagnostics, and executable dynamic reference functions rather than require one canonical trajectory or static answer.
Skill value is a staged treatment: discovery, loading, instruction adherence, and verified outcome must be measured separately because oracle-injected gains collapse under realistic retrieval.
A balanced off/auto/forced/placebo design identifies realized lift, selection tax, and content effect while executor-by-skill interactions distinguish model dependence from loader dependence.
Unique canaries embedded separately in SKILL.md, references, scripts, and assets produce objective exposure receipts and avoid relying on model self-report.

Task success is insufficient test adequacy: only 38.66-45.51% of extracted skill behavior constraints were covered in evaluated trajectories, so skill-benchmark needs constraint coverage alongside outcome lift.
The correct product of skill-benchmark is a versioned effect certificate with a validity domain, not a leaderboard row.

Three randomized arms separate deployable intention-to-treat lift from forced-use capability and expose a diagnostic routing gap; observational activation traces alone cannot make that decomposition causal.
Skill quality is ecology-dependent: discovery degrades with registry complexity, while safety failures can emerge only along composed activation paths.
A two-stage design—fractional-factorial screening followed by exact confirmation of selected interactions—can attribute bundle effects without evaluating the full component power set.

contradictions.claim: GPT Researcher argues that scraping more sites and selecting frequent information reduces incorrect or biased conclusions.
contradictions.counter: This is insufficient when sites copy a common upstream source; the transferable unit is independent claim support, while conflicting or inconclusive evidence must remain explicit.

contradictions.claim: External JSONL state and deterministic resume make a research run replayable.
contradictions.counter: They make the control plane replayable only; web results, page contents, prompts, models, and stochastic reasoning must be versioned or frozen before evidence acquisition and conclusions can be reconstructed.

contradictions.claim: High citation-quality scores demonstrate that a deep-research system is auditable.
contradictions.counter: Current benchmarks score the final report's citations, not search completeness, source-version integrity, inclusion decisions, replay equivalence, or correct propagation of later evidence changes.

contradictions.claim: More citations and broader source diversity imply trustworthy research.
contradictions.counter: Citation-rich systems can remain one-sided, and many statements may still be unsupported by the cited sources; provenance clusters and entailment must be checked separately.

contradictions.claim: An automated claim checker can safely decide truth and convergence.
contradictions.counter: Claim extraction and NLI or LLM checking introduce their own errors. Uncertain cases should abstain or remain quarantined, with raw spans and verifier versions retained for replay.

contradictions.claim: OpenAlex is_retracted can serve as automatic claim-invalidation ground truth.
contradictions.counter: A published comparison reports retraction misclassifications; treat the field as a revalidation trigger and require confirmation from retraction notices, Crossref relations, or publishers.

contradictions.claim: High comment precision or developer acceptance is sufficient evidence that a reviewer is effective.
contradictions.counter: High precision can hide very low recall, while developer action may reflect preference or workflow pressure rather than correctness. Pair fixed human-curated ground truth with fresh online outcomes and report both operating points.

contradictions.claim: More review passes inherently improve review quality.
contradictions.counter: Bounded iteration improved secure-and-correct yield, but gains were uneven at project scope and clearer at narrower scopes; iteration needs adaptive per-finding targeting.

contradictions.claim: A second LLM verdict constitutes independent verification.
contradictions.counter: RepoAudit combines parser-backed ordering checks with LLM path-contradiction analysis; the latter can still share correlated model errors, so executable or formal validators should outrank same-family re-judging.

contradictions.claim: File path plus absolute line range is a suitable primary cross-pass finding identity.
contradictions.counter: SARIF explicitly warns that absolute line numbers destabilize fingerprints after unrelated insertions; location should be supplemented by stable semantic and contextual components.

contradictions.claim: Fresh-context repetition by the same reviewer is sufficient adversarial verification.
contradictions.counter: Fresh context reduces anchoring but does not create tool or evidence independence; CASCADE's false-positive control comes from complementary executions with opposite expected outcomes.

contradictions.claim: Incremental mutation history can be reused whenever the directly mutated file is unchanged.
contradictions.counter: PIT warns that dependency changes can alter behavior without changing the mutated class and that several history optimizations introduce possible error.

contradictions.claim: More context and more requested reasoning should monotonically improve review accuracy.
contradictions.counter: SWE-PRBench reports all eight models degrading from diff-only toward fuller context, while the overcorrection study reports more explanations and proposed corrections increasing misjudgment of correct code.

contradictions.claim: A loop that resolves or reports more issues is necessarily a better reviewer.
contradictions.counter: CR-Bench finds a resolution-versus-spurious-findings frontier and low signal-to-noise when agents are optimized to identify every hidden issue.

contradictions.claim: A syntax-aware diff identifies behavior-preserving changes.
contradictions.counter: Difftastic explicitly compares syntax rather than semantics, treats ordering as significant, and falls back to line diffs on parse failures; AST equality is only a cheap filter.

contradictions.claim: A correct runtime difference is relevant evidence for the reviewed change.
contradictions.counter: Collector-Sahab reported 96% correctness on 29 sampled augmented diffs but only 55% causal relation; timestamps, random paths, and unrelated state can dominate.

contradictions.claim: Failure to establish semantic equivalence demonstrates a behavioral regression.
contradictions.counter: DiffKemp is explicitly incomplete and can report false non-equivalence, especially under complex refactoring; unknown and counterexample must be separate outcomes.

contradictions.claim: Using different model providers or more debate rounds inherently creates useful diversity.
contradictions.counter: Self-MoA can outperform heterogeneous MoA, mathematical MAD often gains little from agent diversity, and homogeneous evidence still induces correlated errors.

contradictions.claim: More rounds monotonically improve the council by increasing convergence.
contradictions.counter: InfoDelphi's third round degraded both calibration and accuracy after private evidence had already propagated; later rounds shifted toward herding.

contradictions.claim: Filtering to the most confident messages is the safest communication policy.
contradictions.counter: Confidence is often miscalibrated; DAR found that retaining authentic disagreement against the majority was more robust than threshold-based uncertainty filtering.

contradictions.claim: Confidence-modulated debate can beat vanilla debate and majority voting when confidence is explicitly calibrated.
contradictions.counter: Raw self-reported confidence weighting produced no benefit for truthfulness, and controlled debate studies found confidence visibility secondary to reasoning strength and diversity; therefore confidence must remain hidden unless calibration is measured out of sample.

contradictions.claim: Additional debate rounds should improve plans through iterative correction.
contradictions.counter: Longer interaction can accumulate identity bias, reduce independent drafting diversity, and degrade performance; round continuation should require verified new evidence or resolved cruxes rather than movement toward agreement.

contradictions.claim: Multi-agent debate is generally superior to a strong direct judge.
contradictions.counter: Debate reliably beat unreliable consultancy, but results against direct answering were mixed on tasks without information asymmetry.

contradictions.claim: A judge that learns during evaluation can safely improve the live council over time.
contradictions.counter: Selective LWE improves pair accuracy and consistency but is explicitly order-sensitive, so live adaptation can make verdicts depend on packet ordering unless calibration is separated and frozen.

contradictions.claim: Exposing calibrated peer confidence can make debate drift toward the correct hypothesis.
contradictions.counter: Perceived expertise, confident rhetoric, and longer arguments can independently increase conformity and reduce representative-agent accuracy.

contradictions.claim: More structured multi-agent deliberation should generally outperform a strong single model.
contradictions.counter: The DCI study reports gains on non-routine and hidden-profile tasks, but the single-agent baseline wins overall and DCI consumes roughly 62 times the tokens.

contradictions.claim: Two-of-three agreement is sufficient evidence that the council resolved the decision.
contradictions.counter: Structured expert elicitation treats discussion as evidence exchange and ambiguity resolution rather than consensus production; post-discussion private estimates and unresolved preference cycles may legitimately remain.

contradictions.claim: A frozen evaluator is sufficient to prevent self-improvement from gaming promotion.
contradictions.counter: A July 2026 preprint reports that even frozen reference-free judges optimize for candidate plausibility when shown the candidate first; blind commitment substantially reduced false positives.

contradictions.claim: The best aggregate candidate should replace the current agent globally.
contradictions.counter: Adaptive Auto-Harness reports that one densely updated harness can peak and decline under heterogeneous, shifting streams; tree-based construction and solve-time routing preserve task-specific benefits.

contradictions.claim: The strongest available model should receive most of the improvement-generation budget.
contradictions.counter: Harness Updating Is Not Harness Benefit reports relatively flat harness-update gains across evolver capability tiers and non-monotonic ability of solver models to benefit from those updates.

contradictions.claim: Freezing one evaluator is sufficient to prevent reward hacking.
contradictions.counter: Black-box bandit optimization can discover semantics-preserving score exploits, and preference attacks can transfer to judges not used during optimization; freezing must be combined with query control, evaluator diversity, adversarial canaries, and periodic human-authorized epoch rotation.

contradictions.claim: A rising aggregate evaluator score proves the candidate is improving.
contradictions.counter: Proxy reward can continue rising after gold reward peaks and declines; conservative ensemble objectives mitigate this better than single-proxy optimization.

contradictions.claim: A weighted aggregate plus fixed dimension floors is an adequate authoritative promotion score.
contradictions.counter: Evolutionary search optimizes whichever scalar is exposed, while heterogeneous dimensions have different noise and calibration; promotion should consume raw paired trials and dimension-level lower bounds, leaving the aggregate for ranking only.

contradictions.claim: Rich evaluator rationales should be returned directly to the candidate generator because they accelerate improvement.
contradictions.counter: Raw rationales are valuable audit evidence but also expose an optimization oracle. Keep them inside the evaluator capsule and return only budgeted, redacted failure classes to mutation workers.
contradictions.evidence: Inference from Autoevals raw metadata retention, DeepEval candidate masking, and Waza's narrow grader interface: https://github.com/braintrustdata/autoevals, https://github.com/confident-ai/deepeval, https://github.com/microsoft/waza

contradictions.claim: Increasing a frozen proxy score makes a candidate increasingly likely to be better.
contradictions.counter: Gold performance can peak and then decline as optimization pressure against an imperfect proxy increases; evaluator query count and candidate fan-out must be treated as risk variables.

contradictions.claim: A different-family LLM grader is sufficient evaluator independence.
contradictions.counter: Different-family grading removes one correlated-bias channel but does not make judgments objective or contamination-free; LiveBench adopted objective ground truth and reports severe judge error on hard tasks.

contradictions.claim: Repeatability evidence is advisory only in the current promotion contract.
contradictions.counter: The shipped promotion path hard-fails when the repeatability report is missing or passed is not true; only weight recommendations are advisory.

contradictions.claim: Dynamic five-dimension profiling provides a frozen evaluator and comparable candidate-minus-baseline deltas.
contradictions.counter: The scorer derives the candidate profile from the candidate and the baseline profile from the baseline, allowing the evaluated check set to change with the subject.

contradictions.claim: Passing a static held-out aggregate is enough to establish deployment robustness.
contradictions.counter: Semantic-invariance and test-awareness studies show that equivalent phrasing or obvious test cues can materially change behavior while conventional benchmark performance remains superficially acceptable.

contradictions.claim: Always reading the live authority gives the strongest verify-first result.
contradictions.counter: Without an immutable authority revision and digest, the same audit cannot be reproduced and an authority can change mid-run; snapshot the live authority at INIT and compare it with head at REPORT.

contradictions.claim: Known deviations should be suppressed so accepted conventions do not appear as findings.
contradictions.counter: Suppression should change the result to an observable SKIP or EXEMPT receipt tied to an exception, not erase the evaluation; otherwise stale exceptions and overbroad matches are invisible.

contradictions.claim: Authority compilation can eliminate reasoning-agent judgment.
contradictions.counter: OPA, TypeSpec, and similar systems compile formal languages, not arbitrary normative prose. Ambiguous obligations must remain explicitly reasoning-required and undergo witness or amendment review rather than being guessed into deterministic rules.

contradictions.claim: Known-deviation suppression reduces noise.
contradictions.counter: Permanent or pre-execution suppression can hide changed conditions and real regressions; InSpec permits optional expiry and can skip a waived control entirely with run=false.

contradictions.claim: The newest negative assertion can safely override earlier findings.
contradictions.counter: Chronology resolves evolution but not authority: deep-alignment must also validate issuer authorization, authority precedence, subject identity, and compatibility before accepting an override.

contradictions.claim: A known deviation should remove or suppress a finding before it reaches persistent state.
contradictions.counter: ORT applies resolutions only during reporting while retaining analyzer and scanner evidence; deep-alignment should likewise append adjudication without deleting the raw fact.

contradictions.claim: Conformance results remain comparable whenever the artifact itself has not changed.
contradictions.counter: Buf exposes several distinct compatibility contracts, showing that an authority change can preserve one dimension while invalidating another; unchanged artifacts may therefore require reclassification or replay.

contradictions.claim: A parsed and type-checked executable rule is sufficient evidence that natural-language authority was compiled correctly.
contradictions.counter: CEL can establish executable validity, but the policy-compilation evidence requires grounding, conflict checks, witnesses, and human correction or approval to establish semantic fidelity.

contradictions.claim: Re-verifying every finding against live ground truth is sufficient for high precision.
contradictions.counter: The same re-probe reproduces stale authority, stale verifier, and shared-model errors; authority and verifier provenance must be independently validated.

contradictions.claim: Checking 100% of discovered artifacts establishes alignment coverage.
contradictions.counter: A faulty discovery adapter or omitted lane shrinks the denominator and can produce a perfect score with silent violations.

contradictions.claim: A cryptographically signed alignment certificate proves that the audited system is conformant or safe.
contradictions.counter: A signature proves origin and integrity of the statement. Authority correctness, scope closure, policy evaluation, evidence sufficiency, and exception validity remain separate obligations.

contradictions.claim: Self-evolving agent systems autonomously produce better agents.
contradictions.counter: Most demonstrate improvement on optimizer-visible proxy tasks; SePO has not released code, while SwarmAgentic explicitly warns that generated code may be destructive. Autonomous score improvement is not a safety or deployment guarantee.

contradictions.claim: The highest aggregate-scoring candidate is the best agent definition.
contradictions.counter: GEPA retains candidates that dominate individual cases or objectives, and AutoPDL finds that optimal prompting strategies vary across models and tasks. Promotion should preserve Pareto alternatives or conditional configurations.

contradictions.claim: Textual gradients provide causal credit assignment.
contradictions.counter: They are LLM-generated diagnoses, not causal proof. The proposed mutation still requires matched counterfactual replay, component ablation, and sealed evaluation before promotion.

contradictions.claim: Pass^3 should be the universal promotion metric.
contradictions.counter: It is appropriate for critical invariants, but applying it to every broad or expensive scenario is cost-heavy and brittle; paired confidence bounds are better for ordinary families.

contradictions.claim: Stored trace replay can replace candidate re-execution.
contradictions.counter: Replay isolates evaluator changes but cannot reveal how a changed agent definition would alter tool selection or control flow; candidates still require fresh paired executions.

contradictions.claim: Executable validators remove the need for semantic judges.
contradictions.counter: They are superior for constraints and side effects, but user satisfaction and semantically equivalent plans still require calibrated semantic evaluation.

contradictions.claim: Scalar validation improvement is sufficient evidence that an agent-package mutation is better.
contradictions.counter: Broad mutations can trade away clean utility, improve only one executor, or repair the wrong locus; promotion needs family vectors, ablation, and transfer evidence.

contradictions.claim: ASB's perturbation results can be reused directly as regression-grade agent-improvement evidence.
contradictions.counter: Its locus taxonomy is valuable, but parts of the runner use substring success checks and an LLM refusal judge; promotion requires deterministic receipts and an isolated verifier.

contradictions.claim: Low variance across repeated benchmark runs demonstrates a robust prompt.
contradictions.counter: A prompt can be perfectly repeatable yet fail badly under semantically equivalent wording; RobustAlpacaEval observed a 45.48-point best-to-worst spread in one model.

contradictions.claim: A held-out aggregate score prevents benchmark overfitting.
contradictions.counter: Held-out data can still encode a narrow proxy, and aggregate performance can hide severe degradation in rare behaviors or other specifications.

contradictions.claim: Improvement on several known agent environments demonstrates general agent capability.
contradictions.counter: AgentRefine reports held-in success alongside failure on unseen environments caused by memorized observation-action relations.

contradictions.claim: An aggregate held-out score improvement is sufficient evidence to promote an agent prompt.
contradictions.counter: Agent definitions contain multiple preserved behavioral families; aggregate lift can hide regressions, and adaptive-evaluation research shows equal accuracy can conceal materially different ability profiles.

contradictions.claim: A judge or human reading the failed trace can reliably identify the responsible step.
contradictions.counter: Trace attribution is correlational; counterfactual intervention is needed to distinguish the visible harmful action from the earlier decision that committed the agent to it.

contradictions.claim: A single aggregate five-dimensional ranking is sufficient for model selection.
contradictions.counter: Prompt-to-Leaderboard finds prompt-dependent Bradley-Terry strengths specifically because marginal rankings conceal prompt-level performance reversals.

contradictions.claim: One judge calibration can be reused across compared candidate models.
contradictions.counter: Cross-model calibration instability can bias the comparison enough to reverse its sign with high apparent confidence.

contradictions.claim: A recent timestamp alone makes a benchmark contamination-free.
contradictions.counter: Temporal filtering lowers risk but does not prove non-exposure; sealed future-resolved questions or private generated cases with explicit exposure lineage provide stronger evidence.

contradictions.claim: A generic public-leaderboard winner is a reliable default model for an unseen workflow.
contradictions.counter: Leaderboard-to-target correlations were below 0.5 for most tested tasks, and the leaderboard winner was best on only one target task.

contradictions.claim: The current model-benchmark lane already performs cost-aware selection.
contradictions.counter: dispatch-model.cjs exposes normalized latency, token, and cost fields, but sweep-benchmark.cjs hardcodes token and cost fields to null and sweep-reporter.cjs ranks efficiency by output word count.

contradictions.claim: Router replay is model-benchmark Mode A and live execution is its Mode B in the current checkout.
contradictions.counter: model-benchmark/MODES.md labels model-vs-model as A and framework bakeoff as B; router-replay versus live execution is documented under skill-benchmark, creating a naming collision.

contradictions.claim: Adaptive testing always improves benchmark efficiency without changing the claim.
contradictions.counter: Candidate-specific item paths destroy direct paired comparability and may omit rare failure families; adaptive trials need common anchors, coverage quotas, and either confirmatory separation or sequentially valid inference.

contradictions.claim: Position swapping should be applied to every LLM-judge comparison.
contradictions.counter: The April 2026 study found position bias small for its tested judges while style bias dominated, making universal swapping a potentially expensive treatment for the wrong measured bias.

contradictions.claim: Quality divided by dollar is a sufficient cost-normalized model score.
contradictions.counter: Ratios are unstable near zero, hide hard quality regressions, and cannot express latency or asymmetric failure costs; quality floors plus Pareto and net-utility sensitivity analysis are safer.

contradictions.claim: A primary and skeptic call constitute two independent reviewers.
contradictions.counter: Both current calls use the same Claude-family grader and shared evidence; different prompts can reveal stance sensitivity but cannot cancel shared self-preference or family bias, and the helper is not wired into the scorer.

contradictions.claim: Controlled degradation creates free gold labels.
contradictions.counter: The label is valid only when the reference is correct and the transformation monotonically worsens the target construct without altering others; otherwise the generated audit probe inherits a gold error.

contradictions.claim: A repeatable benchmark-pass is sufficient promotion evidence.
contradictions.counter: A contaminated fixture, biased judge, collapsed rubric, or wrong oracle can repeat perfectly; repeatability is one validity component, not validity itself.

contradictions.claim: A verified benchmark snapshot can be referenced by name alone.
contradictions.counter: The HLE-Verified arXiv abstract reports 641 verified and 1,170 revised items, while the current repository reports 668 Gold and 1,143 Revision items; snapshot hashes and version-bound counts are necessary.

contradictions.claim: The highest weighted five-dimension score identifies the best model.
contradictions.counter: Small score gaps may be statistically unresolved, and plausible changes to utility weights or task clustering can reverse the winner; the correct result may be a Pareto set, conditional route, or insufficient evidence.

contradictions.claim: A curated task suite is sufficient to predict workflow behavior after switching models.
contradictions.counter: Curated evaluations can be distributionally unrepresentative and recognizable as tests; replaying representative deployment prefixes improved real-deployment forecasts, although environment and tool fidelity remain limiting factors.

contradictions.claim: Broad curated-skill evaluation reports a +16.6 percentage-point aggregate lift.
contradictions.counter: Requirement-driven SWE evaluation reports only +1.2 points on average, with 39 of 49 skills showing zero gain and three showing negative lift; task selection, baseline saturation, specialization, and compatibility therefore dominate any universal skill-effect claim.

contradictions.claim: A standardized Agent Skills folder is portable, so one executor is sufficient for skill evaluation.
contradictions.counter: The standard defines package shape and progressive disclosure, while real harnesses may strip metadata, preload or hide resources, prune instructions, or use different activation mechanisms.

contradictions.claim: A Mode A PASS establishes that the skill is effective.
contradictions.counter: Current Mode A normalizes over router dimensions while task usefulness remains unscored; realistic retrieval experiments show that oracle-route utility can fall toward the no-skill baseline.

contradictions.claim: A signed or verified skill is an effective skill.
contradictions.counter: A signature establishes artifact integrity, not causal efficacy; effectiveness requires separate counterfactual evidence and a validity domain.

contradictions.claim: A skill's isolated benchmark score is sufficient for deployment.
contradictions.counter: Composition can create capability-flow, trust-transfer, and authorization-confusion failures that are absent in isolated arms.

contradictions.claim: One skill-on versus skill-off score explains why value was gained or lost.
contradictions.counter: That comparison estimates an aggregate treatment effect but cannot distinguish discovery, activation, guidance, execution, or component interactions without additional randomized arms.

recommendations.rec: Add a reducer-owned research-plan DAG with typed question nodes, dependencies, perspectives, required source classes, disconfirming queries, budgets, and branch lifecycle events.
recommendations.rationale: It converts linear Next Focus prose into inspectable query planning, supports deterministic branch selection, and makes confirm mode a real plan-revision surface.
recommendations.uniqueness: A durable, revisable investigation plan survives context windows and can be resumed or forked; one-shot research agents expose at most a transient plan.

recommendations.rec: Add a claim-evidence-contradiction ledger and block strong synthesis claims unless they have independent support or an explicit contested/unresolved label.
recommendations.rationale: URL diversity does not establish evidentiary independence; claim-level provenance enables targeted triangulation, contradiction search, and honest abstention.
recommendations.uniqueness: This turns the packet into a longitudinal evidence system that can explain how each conclusion changed and selectively reopen stale or disputed claims.

recommendations.rec: Introduce periodic draft-to-gap passes that audit the evolving outline for unsupported claims, absent counterarguments, stale evidence, and empty sections, then enqueue the highest-value gap.
recommendations.rationale: Search and synthesis otherwise optimize separately; a live draft reveals consequential coverage failures before the budget is exhausted.
recommendations.uniqueness: The final report becomes a reproducible projection of an evolving evidence program rather than a one-time terminal generation.

recommendations.rec: Add an append-only claim-evidence ledger with deterministic claim, query-recipe, and source-version IDs; typed inclusion/exclusion reasons; cited source selectors; support/contradict/context edges; content digests; independence groups; and supersession events.
recommendations.rationale: The current URL citation and iteration summary cannot prove which source version supported which claim or reconstruct why evidence was rejected.
recommendations.uniqueness: Makes deep-research an auditable evidence process whose conclusions can be independently reconstructed, not merely a cited report.

recommendations.rec: Implement living-resume semantics: freeze search recipes, diff new and mutated evidence on resume, invalidate affected claims through dependency edges, emit version-to-version change reports, and export every checkpoint as an RO-Crate linked into the memory graph.
recommendations.rationale: Incremental surveillance turns accumulated state into lower-cost, higher-trust updates instead of repeated full research runs.
recommendations.uniqueness: Creates a durable research dossier that can answer what changed and why months later, which terminal one-shot agents and the inward-looking deep modes do not own.

recommendations.rec: Introduce a logged branch-acquisition scheduler using expected evidence yield, contradiction risk, claim impact, independence gain, staleness, and expected cost; enforce semantic-cluster quotas and deterministic branch-ID tie-breaking, then benchmark pause/resume, source mutation, offline replay, syndicated-source deduplication, and affected-claim detection.
recommendations.rationale: Self-assessed newInfoRatio measures the previous pass but does not choose the next branch by expected research value or verify longitudinal behavior.
recommendations.uniqueness: Sharpens non-converging broadening into explainable novelty-seeking rather than undirected topic expansion.

recommendations.rec: Insert a mode-owned evidence-admission stage before reducer refresh. Resolve source identity, decompose atomic claims, verify entailment against captured spans, cluster derivative sources, scan for retrieved instructions, and emit admit/degrade/quarantine JSONL events.
recommendations.rationale: This prevents fabricated or poisoned iterations from modifying trusted strategy and synthesis while preserving complete forensic history.
recommendations.uniqueness: Turns deep-research into a fault-isolating research process with a recoverable trusted frontier, something a one-shot report cannot expose or resume.

recommendations.rec: Replace convergence input from raw newInfoRatio with trusted evidence yield and STOP blockers for unsupported high-impact claims, unresolved contradiction risk, missing falsification receipts, citation drift, and excessive query-result redundancy. Treat max-iteration exhaustion as incomplete rather than converged.
recommendations.rationale: Low novelty can mean exhaustion, search thrash, checker failure, or poisoning; these states require different recovery and must not share a STOP vote.
recommendations.uniqueness: Makes convergence explainable in research terms: not merely that the loop became repetitive, but that the remaining evidence risk is bounded.

recommendations.rec: Persist a versioned claim-evidence ledger and derive gauges for source-identity pass rate, claim-support coverage, independent-support coverage, query thrash, counterevidence coverage, contamination, and citation drift. Require synthesis to resolve citations from ledger edges.
recommendations.rationale: Iteration prose and URL lists cannot detect paraphrase drift, derivative-source inflation, or changed evidence after resume.
recommendations.uniqueness: Sharpens the moat into living, updateable research: changed or retracted evidence can invalidate only affected claims while preserving the rest of the investigation.

recommendations.rec: Add a reducer-owned ClaimRecord intermediate representation with versioned status, exact evidence locators, support/refute/qualify relations, independence clusters, source versions, and supersession links; emit claim lifecycle events to JSONL and generate research.md from the ledger.
recommendations.rationale: This eliminates silent prose overwrite, makes contradictions explicit, and provides the reverse dependency index needed for targeted refresh.
recommendations.uniqueness: It turns deep-research into a persistent evidence program whose claim history remains inspectable and updateable after the original run—something snapshot research agents and the other deep modes do not own.

recommendations.rec: Insert a pre-synthesis claim-promotion gate that runs separate support and contradiction probes, requires exact passage locators, limits evidence dominance by source cluster, and emits contested or evidence_gap states instead of forcing an answer.
recommendations.rationale: Existing source-diversity gates operate at research-output level; claim-level acceptance catches citation drift, unsupported inference, and buried counterevidence before prose synthesis.
recommendations.uniqueness: Deep-research becomes a system that preserves justified uncertainty and negative knowledge across passes, not merely a longer-form answer generator.

recommendations.rec: Create a two-snapshot longitudinal fixture with additions, corrections, retractions, and ownership-linked duplicate sources; compute affected-claim precision and recall plus unaffected-claim stability before wiring incremental OpenAlex or Crossref refresh.
recommendations.rationale: It directly tests whether targeted refresh is safer and cheaper than full reruns and exposes dependency or source-independence errors before live automation.
recommendations.uniqueness: Passing this benchmark substantiates the living-research moat: the mode can revise exactly what changed while preserving valid prior work.

recommendations.rec: Add a two-lane candidate/proof pipeline. Discovery waves emit FindingCandidate records with semantic cluster key, lineage, model, diff-order seed, and evidence pointers; a separate verifier emits ProofReceipt records. Require proof or independent corroboration for every verdict-affecting P0/P1, not only P0.
recommendations.rationale: This preserves aggressive recall while preventing a single hallucinated lineage from poisoning strongest-restriction fan-in.
recommendations.uniqueness: Deep-review becomes a transparent adversarial adjudicator rather than another multi-agent comment generator.

recommendations.rec: Replace scalar P0/P1/P2 storage with factored impact, confidence, action, evidence_kind, reachability, and lifecycle fields; retain P0/P1/P2 only as a derived presentation projection. Add scoped suppression-memory events with previewed blast radius, expiry/invalidation conditions, and sampled shadow audits.
recommendations.rationale: It separates dangerous-but-unverified hypotheses from confirmed blockers and turns repeated adjudication into controlled institutional knowledge.
recommendations.uniqueness: Externalized, reversible adjudication history can compound across runs without contaminating fresh discovery contexts.

recommendations.rec: Introduce executable finding closure: selectively generate a minimal reproduction or test in an isolated clean worktree, run it against base and head, hash the environment and outputs, then replay the receipt after remediation commits. Benchmark the mode with both seeded test-breaking defects and online resolution/rebuttal outcomes.
recommendations.rationale: A reproducible base/head behavioral delta is stronger evidence than model agreement, while replay closes the loop on incomplete fixes and regressions.
recommendations.uniqueness: This is the moat: a longitudinal, falsifiable release-risk ledger that single-shot reviewers and the other deep modes do not own.

recommendations.rec: Add append-only FindingCandidate, ChallengeAttempt, ProofReceipt, and FindingDisposition events. ProofReceipt should bind the reviewed SHA, verifier-family ID, command/environment digests, immutable input and output hashes, exit code, assertion proved, and independence relationship to the detector. Run verification in a clean checkout with source and baseline tests read-only.
recommendations.rationale: This makes finding-as-hypothesis enforceable, prevents generated reproducers from manufacturing success, and permits deterministic replay and audit.
recommendations.uniqueness: Uniqueness-sharpening: deep-review becomes a producer of revision-bound, independently reproducible defect evidence rather than another stream of LLM comments.

recommendations.rec: Introduce a dimension-coverage ledger keyed by changed surface and applicable review dimension. Schedule fresh-context passes only for uncovered cells, contradicted evidence, or unresolved P0/P1 candidates; require explicit not-applicable reasons and block convergence while required cells remain unresolved.
recommendations.rationale: It converts repeated passes into measurable coverage expansion and prevents apparent convergence caused by every reviewer overlooking the same dimension.
recommendations.uniqueness: Uniqueness-sharpening: unlike generic review bots, deep-review can certify what was examined, what was ruled out, and where residual uncertainty remains.

recommendations.rec: Add a diff-scoped executable-refutation lane for P0/P1 candidates: run existing tests, targeted static queries, line-scoped mutation tests, or generated reproducers in an immutable verifier. Store disprovals in SuppressionMemory scoped by rule, code fingerprint, assumptions, and dependency set; expire suppression when overlapping code or assumptions change.
recommendations.rationale: Cheap mechanical refutation removes false positives before another judge call, while scoped expiry avoids permanently hiding a defect that later becomes reachable.
recommendations.uniqueness: The review loop learns from falsified claims without turning historical false positives into global ignore rules.

recommendations.rec: Add reducer-validated verification_attempt JSONL events and a refute-by-independent-class policy. Run deterministic checks first, require two-sided executable controls for generated reproducers, and classify unresolved support/refutation conflicts as contested.
recommendations.rationale: The current adjudication gate validates packet completeness and self-reported counterevidence, not whether independent falsification actually occurred.
recommendations.uniqueness: Creates a longitudinal proof ledger that single-shot reviewers, static linters, research loops, and planning councils do not maintain.

recommendations.rec: Replace the line-and-description content hash with reducer-owned, versioned partial fingerprints and a confidence-ordered matching cascade; add explicit baseline states and longitudinal dispositions, and invalidate suppressions from symbol, program-slice, and dependency fingerprints.
recommendations.rationale: Line movement, renames, and paraphrased descriptions currently create duplicate findings or unsafe merges, weakening both convergence math and historical suppression.
recommendations.uniqueness: Lets deep-review reason about one defect across many passes and patch revisions instead of generating stateless comments per commit.

recommendations.rec: Introduce risk-triggered coverage cells with evidence-depth states, orthogonal severity/confidence/exploitability fields, and immutable regression receipts requiring pre-fix failure, post-fix success, baseline non-regression, and targeted mutation-survivor adjudication.
recommendations.rationale: A boolean dimension visit cannot distinguish cursory inspection from executable verification, while a green post-fix test alone does not prove that the test detects the original defect.
recommendations.uniqueness: Turns review convergence into release-readiness evidence accumulated across remediation cycles, which is outside the contract of single-shot reviewers and the other read-only deep modes.

recommendations.rec: Add CandidateFindingV2 with separate impact, confidence, evidenceReceipts, verifierOutcome, lifecycleStatus, policyDigest, and subjectDigest. Only verified candidates enter the finding registry; generate the final verdict from that registry and reject narrative or final-line mismatches.
recommendations.rationale: This prevents severity inflation, unsupported P0s, and format gaming without weakening the existing confirmed-P0 failure rule.
recommendations.uniqueness: Uniqueness-sharpening: deep-review becomes an evidence-admission system with revision-spanning finding provenance, not another comment generator.

recommendations.rec: Implement a PassSentinel shadow lane keyed by model, prompt, tools, and context digest. Run matched clean and defective controls, neutral-versus-author-opinion framing, proposed-fix replay, and schema validation; emit healthy, quarantined, or invalid pass health with posterior false-positive risk.
recommendations.rationale: It detects false-positive floods, sycophancy, overcorrection, prompt regressions, and malformed or gamed output before findings contaminate synthesis.
recommendations.uniqueness: Uniqueness-sharpening: the long-running reviewer audits its own reliability online and can suppress an entire bad pass, which single-shot reviewers and static linters cannot do.

recommendations.rec: Replace dimension-only coverage with a dimension-by-reasoning-distance grid and a staged context ladder: changed lines, local program slice, cross-file contract slice, then executable property or reproducer. Mark churn when a pass adds candidates without adding evidence or changing lifecycle state, and sandbox new reviewer policies until shadow calibration passes.
recommendations.rationale: This exposes whole missed bug classes while avoiding the demonstrated failure where indiscriminate context and repeated reflection increase noise.
recommendations.uniqueness: Uniqueness-sharpening: fresh-context iterations become targeted experiments over explicit coverage cells rather than repeated holistic rereads.

recommendations.rec: Build a semantic-obligation planner: normalize the diff, assign stable symbol/AST anchors, expand each residual through call/data-flow slices to observable boundaries, and fan out passes by obligation rather than file. Expose build_obligations(base_sha, head_sha) returning anchor, residual_kind, callers, sinks, tests, unmodeled_boundaries, and confidence.
recommendations.rationale: It removes mechanical review noise and gives multi-pass review an explicit, measurable coverage surface. Start with Difftastic-style AST checks and language adapters; add equality rules and CPG slicing incrementally.
recommendations.uniqueness: This turns deep-review from repeated comment generation into path-accountable semantic change analysis, which single-shot reviewers and linters do not provide.

recommendations.rec: Add a proof-carrying finding ledger with suspected, witnessed, falsified, residual-risk, fixed-pending, fixed-verified, and regressed states. Persist semantic anchors and verifier receipts, require an independent falsifier pass for P0/P1, and render review-report.md as obligations plus evidence plus residual risk.
recommendations.rationale: Fresh-context passes retain independence without rediscovering the same claims, and later patches can be judged as fixes, relocations, or regressions.
recommendations.uniqueness: The durable falsification history is the moat: a PR bot offers a snapshot, while deep-review supplies an evolving assurance argument across patch revisions.

recommendations.rec: Implement an obligation-specific verification ladder: static path or analyzer witness, targeted before/after test, stable runtime-state difference across seeds, mutation or differential test, then scoped semantic-equivalence checking. A P0/P1 must carry an executable counterexample, a sound analyzer path, or an explicit unverified-residual label explaining why verification was unavailable.
recommendations.rationale: It converts plausible findings into reproducible evidence while spending expensive verification only on unresolved high-risk obligations.
recommendations.uniqueness: Deep-review becomes the mode that actively tries to disprove its own findings and verifies fixes against the same witness, beyond both lint rules and one-pass LLM judgment.

recommendations.rec: Add a CouncilBrief compiler producing shared_evidence, per-seat private_evidence, assigned reasoning_path, redundancy_group, prior, and protected objective; choose assignments by evidence-community and structural-path coverage rather than static persona labels.
recommendations.rationale: Designed information asymmetry and path allocation reduce correlated errors before deliberation begins, where later critique cannot reliably repair them.
recommendations.uniqueness: This makes the council a distributed knowledge-reconstruction protocol whose result depends on information no single seat receives, sharply separating it from one-model sampling and the other deep modes.

recommendations.rec: Replace transcript-wide rounds with typed belief messages and recipient-specific dissent retention: immutable claim IDs, probabilities, evidence IDs, atomic derivation steps, challenge targets, and explicit revision causes; trigger external verification when step-level conflicts persist.
recommendations.rationale: It preserves minority evidence, makes persuasion auditable, limits redundant context, and distinguishes evidence-driven belief revision from social conformity.
recommendations.uniqueness: The resulting influence graph and minority lineage are collective-decision artifacts that self-consistency and ordinary synthesis do not produce.

recommendations.rec: Extend the council test gate with single-strong, self-consistency, and same-model MoA control arms plus a constitutional outcome schema containing hard violations, soft objective scores, majority recommendation, minority report, and counterfactual verdicts with each seat removed.
recommendations.rationale: Generic debate is conditionally useful and consensus can be confidently wrong or normatively unacceptable; comparative controls expose whether council structure added value.
recommendations.uniqueness: Positions the mode as plural, accountable decision governance rather than an expensive answer ensemble.

recommendations.rec: Add an independence_batch contract: seal first proposals across CLI-local rounds, persist model-family/reasoning-method/evidence-partition metadata, estimate task-conditioned pairwise error correlations, compute effective_seats, and downgrade runs below an initial N_eff=2 moat floor to ensemble_only.
recommendations.rationale: Nominal seat count is not evidence count; the gate prevents correlated replicas from receiving council authority or budget.
recommendations.uniqueness: This makes demonstrated independent judgment—not multi-agent theater—the admission criterion separating the council from self-consistency sampling.

recommendations.rec: Replace lens-only selection with a sealed diversity auction that chooses seats for marginal reasoning-method, evidence-community, model-family, and tool-surface coverage; preserve the one-CLI-per-round rule by linking isolated rounds under one independence_batch_id.
recommendations.rationale: Distinct cognitive procedures and evidence access create complementary hypotheses; role prose and temperature variation frequently do not.
recommendations.uniqueness: A single model can imitate personas, but it cannot honestly reproduce independent model lineages, disjoint evidence acquisition, and separate tool-derived observations.

recommendations.rec: Introduce blinded trajectory adjudication: anonymous claim IDs, shuffled dual-pass presentation, proposer/critic/verifier/adjudicator separation, content-only scoring before calibrated-confidence reveal, and a minority-sentinel branch that emits factual_uncertainty, objective_tradeoff, hard_veto, or value_disagreement instead of forced consensus.
recommendations.rationale: Identity bias, final-round voting, and correlated LLM judges can erase correct minorities; trajectory evidence and orthogonal verification preserve them.
recommendations.uniqueness: This turns the mode into an auditable pluralist decision institution capable of preserving and adjudicating legitimate disagreement—work neither a single answer nor the other deep modes perform.

recommendations.rec: [uniqueness-sharpening] Add a typed adjudication router with four terminal semantics: reliability-weighted factual posterior, blinded pairwise plan posterior, evidence-focused debate escalation, and preserved plural/value disagreement.
recommendations.rationale: No single aggregation rule is valid across truth claims, comparative plans, constitutional constraints, and values; routing prevents majority vote from manufacturing consensus.
recommendations.uniqueness: Turns the mode into an auditable decision institution that chooses how to disagree, something single-model self-consistency and the evidence-producing deep modes do not provide.

recommendations.rec: Implement a packet-external hierarchical reliability ledger and an active-seat selector scoring expected posterior entropy reduction times decision impact divided by token and latency cost.
recommendations.rationale: It replaces equal seat weight and fixed rosters with calibrated conditional competence while avoiding repeated calls to correlated or uninformative seats.
recommendations.uniqueness: Sharpens the council into a conditional-competence market whose diversity has measured marginal value rather than cosmetic persona variety.

recommendations.rec: Add a council adjudicator gate that requires anonymous candidates, A/B and B/A judging, provider and confidence masking, bandwagon concealment, OffsetBias-style adversarial probes, and a frozen judge-state hash.
recommendations.rationale: Position, verbosity, authority, nested-instruction, and sequential-learning effects can dominate substantive evidence unless verdict invariance is measured directly.
recommendations.uniqueness: Produces counterfactual receipts showing that the verdict survives changes irrelevant to merit, making council decisions more defensible than a strong model's unobservable internal judgment.

recommendations.rec: [UNIQUENESS] Add an evidence-conditioned stance-transition ledger: seal each initial stance, confidence, claim set, and falsifier; require later changes to cite adopted claim IDs; persist unsupported-majority-flip and minority-erasure gauges.
recommendations.rationale: This separates evidence transfer from social imitation and converts false consensus into an auditable failure state.
recommendations.uniqueness: A single model or same-model self-consistency cannot provide a cross-vantage causal record showing who changed, why, and whether dissent was answered.

recommendations.rec: Require a counterfactual convergence certificate on flagged rounds: anonymize sources, normalize length, reverse candidate order, permute aliases, hide majority counts, and fail convergence if the material verdict changes.
recommendations.rationale: The intervention directly tests position, verbosity, authority, bandwagon, and dominant-speaker sensitivity while keeping extra judge cost conditional.
recommendations.uniqueness: It turns council deliberation into a falsifiable social-influence experiment rather than a more expensive vote.

recommendations.rec: Extend the council test gate with BenchForm-style Wrong Guidance, Trust, and Doubt cases plus malicious-seat, verbosity, identity-authority, and cloned-seat variants; compare council marginal value against equal-token single-model, self-consistency, majority-vote, and verifier-guided best-of-N baselines.
recommendations.rationale: Prompt-level anti-groupthink claims are not credible until the complete protocol resists known social-pressure treatments and still adds value over cheaper alternatives.
recommendations.uniqueness: The gate makes social robustness and dissent recovery part of the mode's product contract.

recommendations.rec: Add a council-worthiness preflight to deep_ai_council_config.json with distributed_information, value_conflict, decision_materiality, process_accountability, cheapest_baseline, and admission_reason; bypass the council for routine or single-answer work unless the caller explicitly requests procedural accountability.
recommendations.rationale: This is the best impact-to-effort change: it avoids the mode's large cost where evidence shows no advantage and concentrates evaluation on tasks where multiple partial perspectives can change the result.
recommendations.uniqueness: It defines the moat as accountable integration of distributed information and legitimate value conflict, not generic multi-agent sampling.

recommendations.rec: Extend prompt_pack_round.md, output_schema.md, and persist-artifacts.cjs with private pre/post belief records, typed epistemic moves, pairwise ballots with ties, and a plural verdict containing factual beliefs, dominated options, unbeaten set, vetoes, minority evidence, unresolved values, and reopen conditions; make these sections strict for full council runs.
recommendations.rationale: The current 100-point rubric and two-of-three signal can hide incomparable values and social conformity. Typed moves plus plural aggregation preserve the reason a council exists.
recommendations.uniqueness: A single model can recommend an option; this artifact proves which independent beliefs, attacks, value conflicts, and residual objections survived deliberation.

recommendations.rec: Add a counterfactual robustness suite under scripts/tests and the manual testing playbook: fork before cross-seat exposure, perturb seat identity, response order, argument length, claimed expertise, majority count, and trusted history one at a time, then gate on evidence-invariant verdict stability and minority-evidence retention.
recommendations.rationale: A council cannot claim independent deliberative value if superficial social cues determine its winner. Paired forks isolate those effects more cleanly than aggregate convergence gauges.
recommendations.uniqueness: It makes the council's claimed advantage—robust integration across genuinely distinct seats—falsifiable at the mode test gate.

recommendations.rec: Implement a CandidateMatrixArchive with candidate_proposed, candidate_evaluated, and promotion_receipt JSONL events carrying lineage, mutation-operator ID, per-case and five-dimension vectors, evaluator/split digests, artifacts, cost, and promotion scope; filter by minibatch non-regression before full held-out evaluation.
recommendations.rationale: This converts five-dimensional scoring from a terminal ranking into a searchable evidence surface while preserving complementary candidates and controlling evaluation cost.
recommendations.uniqueness: Deep-improvement becomes the only mode that can bind a concrete agent mutation to reproducible evaluation evidence and an auditable promotion receipt.

recommendations.rec: Add a budgeted mutation-strategy portfolio covering trace-reflective mutation, program/data-aware proposal, crossover, workspace architecture search, and mutation-policy meta-evolution; allocate trials by held-out improvement per unit cost while cryptographically freezing evaluator assets.
recommendations.rationale: GEPA, MIPRO, ADAS, Promptbreeder, and STOP show that candidate quality depends heavily on the proposal mechanism and that the proposal mechanism can improve recursively.
recommendations.uniqueness: This sharpens the moat into controlled recursive improvement: the generator may rewrite how it generates, but it can never rewrite or impersonate promotion authority.

recommendations.rec: Use two-key, profile-scoped promotion: a candidate-blind hidden-anchor/verifier channel plus the frozen five-dimension evaluator; globally promote only after cross-profile transfer, otherwise retain the candidate as a routed specialist with baseline fallback.
recommendations.rationale: Frozen reference-free judges remain vulnerable to plausibility optimization, while a single globally updated harness can degrade on heterogeneous task streams.
recommendations.uniqueness: Naive self-improvement produces one self-endorsed replacement; deep-improvement produces bounded, reversible, workload-scoped capabilities whose acceptance does not depend on the candidate's own persuasion.

recommendations.rec: Add a lane-independent EvaluatorCapsule created before candidate generation.
