Hash evaluator code, rubric, real judge build identifiers, aggregation policy, gold anchors, and a commitment to hidden fixtures; record the capsule in session_start and require a matching signed EvalReceipt at every promotion gate. Run the evaluator in a separate capability boundary, expose no hidden paths, and cap promotion-oracle queries per candidate.
recommendations.rationale: Lane D already freezes part of its scoring surface, but the guarantee is not mode-wide and one benchmark cache still uses a placeholder grader identity. Hashing plus reachability isolation prevents ruler mutation; query budgeting reduces black-box ruler extraction.
recommendations.uniqueness: This makes deep-improvement the only mode that commits the ruler before proposing a mutation and can prove that the mutation never controlled or inspected its promotion oracle.

recommendations.rec: Add an adversarial conservative evaluator quorum for finalists: deterministic executable anchors first, then at least two provider/family-diverse judges and one attack lane. Require non-negative per-judge lower confidence bounds on safety dimensions, use uncertainty-weighted aggregation for utility, and return inconclusive on excessive disagreement. Run BITE-style semantic-preserving edits and generated Giskard scenarios against the evaluator capsule; refresh attacks only between frozen epochs.
recommendations.rationale: Different-family grading removes one correlation but does not prevent transferable style attacks or shared judge biases. Conservative aggregation and adversarial meta-evaluation make disagreement and evaluator fragility promotion blockers.
recommendations.uniqueness: The mode improves not only the candidate but the credibility of the evidence authorizing promotion, while keeping evaluator evolution temporally separated from candidate optimization.

recommendations.rec: Extend promote-candidate from accept/ship to accept-offline/shadow/canary/ship. Shadow runs paired baseline and candidate on redacted trace snapshots with candidate outputs discarded. Canary adapters expose 1%, 5%, then 20% of eligible traffic with pauses and automatically restore the baseline on a hard-rule failure, negative evaluator lower bound, cost ceiling, or error-rate regression. Journal only trace references, hashes, metrics, and stage decisions.
recommendations.rationale: Offline fixtures cannot prove compatibility with the operating distribution. Progressive exposure supplies paired downstream evidence while limiting blast radius and preserving deterministic rollback.
recommendations.uniqueness: This turns deep-improvement from guarded prompt editing into a progressive-delivery controller for agent behavior, a capability absent from research, review, council, and alignment modes.

recommendations.rec: Add an append-only TrialScore ledger and split grading, calibration, reduction, and promotion into separately versioned stages. Preserve pre-reduction values; calibrate ordinal judges against gold anchors; compute paired per-dimension lower bounds; prohibit aggregate compensation for critical-dimension regressions.
recommendations.rationale: The current rounded weighted score cannot be recalibrated or audited without rerunning candidates, and its aggregate may hide dimension-specific deterioration.
recommendations.uniqueness: Turns deep-improvement into an auditable measurement authority rather than another prompt optimizer.

recommendations.rec: Replace single-candidate generation with a budgeted portfolio of targeted reflection, single-slot mutation, crossover, and novelty restart; retain a profile-scoped Pareto archive and evaluate every survivor through order-swapped blind comparison plus metamorphic relations.
recommendations.rationale: Evolution broadens the search while vector-aware survival and relation gates prevent scalar reward hacking.
recommendations.uniqueness: Combines broad mutation with a frozen evaluator that candidates cannot inspect or overrule.

recommendations.rec: Insert a signed shadowing state between accepted and ship_eligible: replay sampled real packets read-only against the incumbent, commit evaluator and fixture digests before viewing outputs, and gate on paired dimension deltas, metamorphic violations, judge disagreement, and tail cost.
recommendations.rationale: Offline held-out success is necessary but does not establish performance on the live workload distribution; shadow receipts provide a reversible predictive bridge.
recommendations.uniqueness: This is the moat-sharpening promotion transaction: only deep-improvement can mutate an agent and then earn canonicalization through hidden, frozen, staged evidence.

recommendations.rec: Implement EvaluatorReceiptV2 and require promote-candidate.cjs to verify its signature, candidate/baseline/evaluator hashes, fresh canary epoch, gold calibration, query count, and empty veto set; make sealed/gold regression and evaluator-surface mutation non-overridable.
recommendations.rationale: This closes forged receipts, stale evidence, reward/test tampering, and same-session rubric changes while keeping exact evaluator internals outside the mutator's capability boundary.
recommendations.uniqueness: Makes deep-improvement the only mode that can issue cryptographically bound mutation authority after independent falsification, not merely advice or a higher score.

recommendations.rec: Add a non-extractive evaluator-oracle API: Ladder-style material-improvement updates, coarse verdict bands, per-epoch query budgets, terminal-only exact evidence, semantic leak scanning across candidate/proposer-visible artifacts, and automatic canary-epoch burn on any match.
recommendations.rationale: Repeated dimension-level feedback lets the optimizer reconstruct the promotion surface even when fixture files are hidden; coarse thresholded feedback and semantic scanning reduce that channel.
recommendations.uniqueness: Sharpens the frozen-evaluator moat by making evaluator secrecy an enforced information-flow property rather than an operator convention.

recommendations.rec: Replace aggregate-only promotion with a regression envelope: paired global lower bound, non-overridable sealed/gold slice floors, automatic weak-segment discovery, action-trace drift testing, and proxy-versus-sealed gain curves indexed by evaluator query count.
recommendations.rationale: Aggregate gains can conceal mode collapse, tail regressions, and behavior drift; the envelope turns each into a named promotion veto and stops overoptimization before the proxy/gold relationship reverses.

recommendations.rec: Add freeze-evaluator.cjs at INIT to emit EvaluatorCapsuleV3 from the canonical target; make score-candidate.cjs require --capsule for baseline deltas and score both subjects with one frozen profile; bind capsuleHash into cache keys, journal events, accept/ship receipts, and reject cross-evaluatorEpoch comparisons.
recommendations.rationale: This closes the confirmed profile-substitution gap while retaining target-specific dynamic profiling.
recommendations.uniqueness: It converts evaluator-first from a workflow convention into a mechanically enforced separation between mutator and ruler—the core advantage over naive self-improvement.

recommendations.rec: Add a rule-delta ledger and proposal filter: canonicalize semantic additions, classify them as GENERALIZED_RULE, CASE_PATCH, or STYLE_ONLY, count support across distinct failure families, and rank candidates by held-out delta minus capsule-frozen capacity and scope-narrowness penalties.
recommendations.rationale: It suppresses prompt bloat, example memorization, and cosmetically different candidates before costly graders and promotion gates.
recommendations.uniqueness: Deep-improvement can prove that a change generalizes across evidence families; a self-rewriter optimizing its visible score cannot distinguish broad rules from grader-specific patches reliably.

recommendations.rec: Add run-metamorphic-gate.cjs plus a relation registry whose records contain precondition, generator, transform, comparator, visibility, risk tier, and shrinker; include semantic-invariance, identifier-equivariance, tool-trace, and real-versus-test-context families; emit PASS/BREACH/INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE per family and require aggregate GO for canonical promotion.
recommendations.rationale: This deepens the prior cheap metamorphic recommendation into a reproducible, minimizable behavioral proof and prevents missing fixtures from being treated as non-regression.
recommendations.uniqueness: Hidden relational tests let deep-improvement validate behavior the proposer cannot directly optimize or redefine, yielding a stronger promotion moat than static scoring.

recommendations.rec: Add compileAuthority(ref) and an immutable AuthorityCapsule containing revision, sourceDigest, compilerVersion, typed rules with source spans and modalities, compile diagnostics, capability profile, rule-test coverage, and signature. standardSource() should return a capsule reference; findings are forbidden when the capsule is invalid, stale, or has unacknowledged uncompiled obligations.
recommendations.rationale: The current adapter validates artifacts against paths and reasoning packets but does not prove that the authority was completely or consistently translated.
recommendations.uniqueness: This makes deep-alignment a compiler-backed certifier of named authorities rather than another collection of lint rules.

recommendations.rec: Require a witness matrix per compiled rule: conforming example, violating example, boundary cases, and relational/stateful sequences where applicable. Shrink failures and store capsuleDigest, ruleId, witnessHash, evidenceHash, result, and replay command in each decision receipt; add rule and branch coverage gauges beside artifact coverage.
recommendations.rationale: Artifact coverage can reach 100% while important authority branches were never exercised; minimized witnesses make verify-first findings independently reproducible.
recommendations.uniqueness: Replayable semantic proofs are stronger than a linter diagnostic or an LLM assertion and remain comparable across iterations.

recommendations.rec: Replace passive known-deviation entries with governed exceptions carrying owner, issuedAt, expiresAt, authorityDigest, ruleRefs, scopePredicate, justification evidence, and reportResult. On authority changes, replay prior witnesses, classify added/removed/weakened obligations, require an amendment decision, and report cross-lane authority conflicts separately from artifact failures.
recommendations.rationale: Exceptions otherwise become permanent blind spots, while current-only audits cannot detect standards weakened to make existing artifacts pass.
recommendations.uniqueness: Temporal authority governance plus multi-authority conflict analysis is the clearest moat over linters, one-shot prompts, deep-review, and mutation-oriented deep-improvement.

recommendations.rec: Add immutable AuthorityCapsules and proof-carrying AuditDeterminations. Require authority URI, edition and digest, stable claim anchor, lane, precedence, applicability predicate, required evidence classes, verifier contract, subject digest, observations, and a six-state verdict including inconclusive and authority_conflict.
recommendations.rationale: This prevents prompt interpretation from silently becoming authority and makes every conformance claim replayable, reviewable, and invalidatable.
recommendations.uniqueness: This turns deep-alignment into authority-bound adjudication; linters emit matches and one-shot prompts cannot maintain a durable authority-to-evidence proof chain.

recommendations.rec: Replace pre-verification suppression with exact, expiring DeviationAssertions. Match on authority digest, claim, lane, semantic subject fingerprint, applicability predicate, verifier version, and deviation kind; verify first, then classify as accepted_deviation. Persist issuer, approver, evidence, timestamps, expiry, revocation, and invalidation reasons as JSONL events.
recommendations.rationale: Precise post-verification disposition prevents broad ignores, reveals when a deviation disappears, and automatically reopens stale exceptions after relevant drift.
recommendations.uniqueness: A versioned negative-assertion ledger gives deep-alignment exception-aware longitudinal judgment that ordinary linters and prompts do not provide.

recommendations.rec: Create a per-lane verifier admission and observability gate: shadow-run new authority/verifier pairs, sample dispositions, model the effective-false-positive posterior, and promote only when its upper confidence bound clears the configured lane threshold. Publish evidence coverage, inconclusive rate, stale deviations, suppression leakage, and authority-conflict counts.
recommendations.rationale: Verify-first is credible only if the system measures whether verified findings remain useful and whether abstention or suppression is hiding coverage gaps.
recommendations.uniqueness: It makes audit trust an evidence-backed operational property rather than a prompt-quality claim.

recommendations.rec: Add an Authority Capsule Compiler that emits stable rule IDs, source spans, typed applicability and assertion expressions, evidence policies, exceptions, witnesses, and a capsule digest; require static validation plus delete, weaken, invert, narrow, and exception-wrap mutation tests before publication.
recommendations.rationale: This closes the unverified semantic gap between an authority document and executable checks, while making authority changes diffable and replayable.
recommendations.uniqueness: It turns deep-alignment from a rule runner into a governed compiler for evolving normative authority, a capability absent from ordinary linters and one-shot prompts.

recommendations.rec: Require content-bound EvidenceReceipt events for findings and represent deviations as append-only adjudication records with expiry, authority compatibility ranges, verifier-version triggers, and EXACT/MIGRATABLE/STALE/AMBIGUOUS matching.
recommendations.rationale: Raw observations remain auditable, stale suppressions reopen automatically, and absence or verifier failure cannot silently become conformity.
recommendations.uniqueness: The resulting conformance ledger can explain exactly why a historical finding was active or suppressed at any authority epoch.

recommendations.rec: Separate stable logical lane IDs from epoch-specific lane IDs, classify authority diffs by compatibility type, construct rule-to-observation-to-artifact impact components, and replay only affected witnesses, deviations, and artifacts.
recommendations.rationale: This gives authority upgrades deterministic blast-radius analysis without paying for a full audit or claiming false comparability across editions.
recommendations.uniqueness: It enables counterfactual and longitudinal answers such as whether a failure came from artifact drift, authority drift, verifier drift, or environment drift.

recommendations.rec: Add an authority-capsule gate before DISCOVER with source digests, rule manifest, publisher, authority epoch, verifier digest, expiry, predecessor epoch, and rollback or mix-and-match rejection.
recommendations.rationale: Verify-first cannot establish precision when the authority or verifier being re-probed is stale, partial, or unauthorized.
recommendations.uniqueness: Turns alignment into a reproducible temporal conformance proof tied to an authorized standard version, which a linter or one-shot prompt cannot provide.

recommendations.rec: Compile known deviations into an expiring typed ledger and add an unsuppressed shadow lane, with full replay after authority or verifier changes and cardinality checks after artifact moves.
recommendations.rationale: This detects stale exceptions, selector widening, verifier pinning, and suppression leakage without sacrificing the default report's precision.
recommendations.uniqueness: Makes exception governance and drift-through-suppression first-class longitudinal evidence rather than a static ignore list.

recommendations.rec: Require a rule-applicability closure matrix plus positive, negative, and metamorphic sentinels; classify rules as blocking or candidate and prohibit PASS when a sentinel survives or an applicability edge is uncovered.
recommendations.rationale: Artifact coverage alone cannot detect broken discovery, missing lanes, or verifier false negatives.
recommendations.uniqueness: The resulting alignment certificate explains why absence of findings is credible, separating deep-alignment from finding-oriented linters and general deep-review.

recommendations.rec: Implement Alignment Case Certificate v0 as canonical JSON: authority capsule, subject-set digest, verifier capsule, lane-closure manifests, per-obligation assertions with supporting and counter-evidence, exception predicates, invalidation dependencies, and a replay command that returns an identical assertion vector or explicit UNREPLAYABLE reasons.
recommendations.rationale: This closes the largest trust gap between a reported PASS and a result that can be independently reproduced after authority, artifact, verifier, or environment changes.
recommendations.uniqueness: It turns deep-alignment into a proof-carrying longitudinal audit whose authority, coverage, witnesses, exceptions, and revocation conditions are inspectable—capabilities absent from linters, one-shot prompts, and the other deep modes.

recommendations.rec: Build a semantic invalidation graph and typed delta engine. Link every conclusion to rule AST nodes, logical subjects, evidence, discovery adapters, verifier semantics, environment facts, and exception conditions; propagate PRESERVED, REPLAY_REQUIRED, REVIEW_REQUIRED, or INVALID after each delta.
recommendations.rationale: It prevents both stale suppression and expensive whole-repository re-audits, while making authority upgrades and verifier changes explainable.
recommendations.uniqueness: The mode becomes an authority-aware conformance history engine, not another stateless checker.

recommendations.rec: Require a closure proof before lane PASS: compare authoritative and independent discovery manifests, account for every difference, and use PASS, FAIL, CANNOT_TELL, INAPPLICABLE, UNTESTED, and EXEMPT dispositions with recorded test mode. Treat unexplained scope differences and CANNOT_TELL as non-passing.
recommendations.rationale: Finding quality is irrelevant when files, generated artifacts, or whole artifact classes are absent from the audited universe.
recommendations.uniqueness: Provable obligation and subject coverage is the clearest moat over a linter or one-shot prompt, both of which ordinarily reason only over what they happened to receive.

recommendations.rec: Implement a typed AgentIR and causal-slicing evaluator: parse every agent definition into named mutable components, map failed traces to implicated component IDs, and allow only schema-valid typed patch operations.
recommendations.rationale: This converts whole-file prompt rewriting into localized, reviewable, causally testable optimization across prompts, tools, topology, memory, and inference settings.
recommendations.uniqueness: Establishes agent-improvement as a behavioral compiler rather than another critique or prompt-editing loop.

recommendations.rec: Replace single-score candidate selection with blocker-aware Pareto successive halving: cheap invariant and replay checks first, growing development batches second, stochastic stress trials third, and a sealed promotion set last.
recommendations.rationale: It preserves complementary behavior strategies, controls rollout cost, and prevents correctness gains from compensating for discipline or safety regressions.
recommendations.uniqueness: Produces evidence-backed promotion decisions that manual prompt editing and judgment-only deep modes cannot issue.

recommendations.rec: Add an independently versioned improver-agent lane trained on historical agent failures, with its own train/dev/sealed benchmarks and a frozen optimizer version recorded in every candidate lineage.
recommendations.rationale: The mutation policy can accumulate transferable diagnosis skill without recursively changing during an experiment or contaminating the promotion judge.
recommendations.uniqueness: Makes improvement capability itself a benchmarked reusable asset while preserving an external promotion authority.

recommendations.rec: Build a contract-to-benchmark compiler that parses normative agent clauses into typed obligations and reports clause, authority-conflict, transition, side-effect, negative-capability, and perturbation coverage.
recommendations.rationale: The current aggregate scoring can improve while entire classes of behavioral obligations remain untested.
recommendations.uniqueness: Turns an AGENT definition into an executable behavioral contract, which manual editing and the non-improvement deep modes cannot certify.

recommendations.rec: Add a versioned discipline stress suite with act/refuse/clarify families, authority-conflict injections, tool and state failures, side-effect oracles, and metamorphic variants generated from every seed case.
recommendations.rationale: This exposes regressions hidden by ordinary happy-path tasks and permits parallel execution by behavior family without collapsing distinct failure modes.
recommendations.uniqueness: Measures whether the agent remains disciplined under pressure, not merely whether its edited prompt sounds clearer or completes familiar examples.

recommendations.rec: Introduce a content-addressed behavioral compatibility certificate and staged proposed-to-shadow-to-canary-to-promoted lifecycle, with critical Pass^3 gates, paired family deltas, sealed canaries, evaluator and environment digests, and an explicit rollback reference.
recommendations.rationale: Promotion becomes reproducible and reversible while preventing aggregate-score gains from masking family regressions or lucky executions.
recommendations.uniqueness: Makes agent-improvement an agent-definition CI/CD system whose output is a verifiable promotion decision rather than a preferred prompt draft.

recommendations.rec: Add a typed BehaviorExperiment layer with known-locus defect injection, clean and perturbed variants, receipt predicates, causal-blame scoring, and collateral-utility measurements.
recommendations.rationale: It distinguishes a genuine localized repair from an accidental aggregate-score improvement and directly closes the open causal blame-assignment thread.
recommendations.uniqueness: This turns agent-improvement into a controlled behavioral laboratory; manual editing and the other modes do not connect known interventions to attributable agent-definition repairs.

recommendations.rec: Implement failure-clustered textual-gradient proposals as single-locus agent-package diffs, then retain a top-k Pareto frontier with patch ablations before allowing recombination.
recommendations.rationale: It adds useful reflective and evolutionary search without losing attribution to broad multi-clause mutations or collapsing tradeoffs into one scalar.
recommendations.uniqueness: The mode evolves versioned agent programs while preserving clause-level causal lineage, rather than producing another optimized prompt string.

recommendations.rec: Require a sealed promotion lane that replays candidates through at least one non-discovery executor family, uses an isolated verifier, and emits a hash-bound behavioral transfer certificate with a rollback parent.
recommendations.rationale: Executor-specific prompt wins and verifier leakage otherwise masquerade as durable agent improvements.
recommendations.uniqueness: The resulting artifact is a portable behavioral warranty, which is stronger and more operationally valuable than vibes-based prompt approval or a generic improvement report.

recommendations.rec: Add a four-ring evaluation manifest: visible optimizer fixtures, sealed semantic variants, untouched-family sentinels, and rotating canaries. Record exposure epoch and retire any hidden fixture after proposer-visible failure disclosure.
recommendations.rationale: This distinguishes ordinary holdout performance from semantic robustness, capability retention, and contamination-resistant validation.
recommendations.uniqueness: Turns agent-improvement into a blind, governed behavioral experiment rather than an automated form of prompt tweaking.

recommendations.rec: Replace aggregate-only promotion with family-level retention contracts and three-state stability. Missing samples are insufficientEvidence; critical families use all-samples-pass; other families require candidate lower-bound delta above a configured negative epsilon and bounded worst-variant loss.
recommendations.rationale: Blocks capability regressions that Pareto and mean-score gates miss, while preventing absent or all-zero evidence from appearing stable.

recommendations.rec: Build patch-causal evaluation: compile inherited agent clauses into clean, semantic-variant, authority-conflict, shadowing, and hostile-override scenarios; default to one clause mutation per candidate and ablate multi-clause winners before promotion. Emit behavioral-semver impact from changed and regressed families.
recommendations.rationale: Connects behavioral deltas to specific instruction clauses and makes rollback, review, and transfer across executors defensible.
recommendations.uniqueness: This causal clause-to-behavior-to-promotion chain is the clearest moat over manual editing and every read-only or deliberative deep mode.

recommendations.rec: Add compile-agent-change producing change-contract.json, static assertions, trace policies, generated scenarios, and a patch/minor/major promotion matrix from the base, candidate, and inheritance graph.
recommendations.rationale: It converts prose diffs into testable intended and preserved behavior, closes the inherited-clause gap, and makes promotion scope explicit.
recommendations.uniqueness: Turns agent-improvement into behavioral change control for agent constitutions rather than another scalar prompt optimizer.

recommendations.rec: Implement a constrained adaptive promotion allocator with mandatory family/executor sentinels, paired-delta IRT calibration, time-uniform non-inferiority bounds, and a canary exposure/retirement ledger.
recommendations.rationale: It reduces the benchmark matrix while preserving causal comparison, transfer coverage, and contamination-aware evidence validity.
recommendations.uniqueness: Produces a defensible transfer envelope and evidence freshness record that manual tweaking and generic prompt optimization do not provide.

recommendations.rec: Add failure-triggered counterfactual replay using changed clauses as do_context interventions and executors as do_policy interventions, escalating to budgeted Shapley attribution only for unresolved interactions.
recommendations.rationale: It separates candidate-caused regressions from executor variance and localizes the smallest repair surface before another mutation.
recommendations.uniqueness: Makes the mode an evidence-producing causal debugger for agent-definition changes, not only an evaluator.

recommendations.rec: Add BenchmarkDesign and TrialResult events implementing paired task blocks, randomized path/order, crossed model-path factors, repeated seeds where needed, clustered bootstrap intervals, and dimension-specific non-inferiority promotion gates.
recommendations.rationale: This separates real treatment effects from task, sampling, and path variance and prevents a weighted point score from promoting an uncertain or confounded winner.
recommendations.uniqueness: It makes the mode a causal stack-selection experiment rather than another score-producing leaderboard.

recommendations.rec: Insert a versioned judge-calibration firewall: deterministic checks first, blinded mirrored pairwise judgments, style-feature controls, a task- and candidate-specific oracle slice, prediction-powered correction, calibration-aware intervals, and explicit abstention.
recommendations.rationale: Judge disagreement is measurement error, not ordinary model variance; uncorrected or shared calibration can reverse the selected model.

recommendations.rec: Build a sealed task-lineage compiler from current workflow traces and validated counterfactuals, then learn prompt/task-conditioned Bradley-Terry strengths and emit a versioned cost-aware routing policy rather than a global winner.
recommendations.rationale: Private current tasks, objective receipts, contamination lineage, and local cost constraints are precisely the evidence public leaderboards and manual comparisons cannot supply.
recommendations.uniqueness: This turns model-benchmark into the only deep mode that answers which complete executor policy should handle each exact evolving task family and updates the live router from controlled evidence.

recommendations.rec: Add a Private Workload Contract and executionPath axis to sweep-benchmark.cjs: freeze a stratified workload snapshot with task cluster, traffic weight, failure severity, oracle_ref, source cutoff, and exposure state; expand identical task IDs across router-replay, router-live, and direct-live paths; report public/private rank correlation, public-top-1 regret, route regret, and costly-slice failures.
recommendations.rationale: Paired own-task evidence answers the deployment question that a public aggregate cannot, while retaining public leaderboards as inexpensive shortlist priors.
recommendations.uniqueness: UNIQUENESS-SHARPENING: makes proprietary workload regret recovered by in-workflow evaluation the mode's defining output, which public leaderboards and the other deep modes cannot observe.

recommendations.rec: Implement a sealed-canary JSONL lifecycle with creation hash, source timestamp, proposer and oracle visibility, evaluation count, disclosure, retirement, and replacement lineage; add invariant protocol siblings and select on quality lower bound plus protocol volatility or worst slice.
recommendations.rationale: It reduces memorization and evaluator leakage while preserving enough lineage to reproduce decisions and replace disclosed cases.
recommendations.uniqueness: Private, expiring, task-derived canaries create a defensible evidence asset unavailable to public benchmarks and manual comparisons.

recommendations.rec: Wire dispatch-model.cjs usage fields through dispatchCell and every sweep row, including failures; aggregate inference, routing, retry, grader, and cache-adjusted cost plus p95 latency; choose the cheapest candidate whose paired quality lower bound and latency satisfy profile gates, expose the Pareto set, and stop sampling statistically dominated cells after a configurable minimum.
recommendations.rationale: The checkout already has normalized usage fields and paired-bootstrap statistics, but the sweep currently discards usage and substitutes output length for efficiency.
recommendations.uniqueness: Turns benchmark quality into an operational deployment decision under the user's actual prices, retries, routing overhead, and SLOs.

recommendations.rec: Add a versioned BenchmarkTrial JSONL schema and item-family registry containing common anchors, perturbation lineage, deterministic receipts, contamination evidence, judge identity, and normalized usage; propagate the dispatch envelope's existing token and cost fields into sweep rows.
recommendations.rationale: The present aggregate rows cannot support clustered inference, resume-safe evidence lineage, contamination auditing, or real cost-aware selection, and the runner currently discards usage already parsed by the dispatcher.
recommendations.uniqueness: Creates private, auditable task evidence that public leaderboards and one-off comparisons cannot observe or reconstruct.

recommendations.rec: Replace the top-pair flat 90% bootstrap gate with a confirmatory engine: common-item pairing, task-family nested bootstrap, McNemar for binary outcomes, Holm-controlled promotion tests, per-dimension non-inferiority floors, stratified PPI judge correction, and Bradley-Terry or Davidson rank uncertainty.
recommendations.rationale: This prevents false promotion from correlated perturbations, multiple comparisons, judge measurement error, presentation order, and rank instability.

recommendations.rec: Make the terminal artifact a task-conditional selection policy with preferred model, fallback, confidence, evidence expiry, hard-floor status, Pareto position, and sensitivity to cost, latency, traffic mix, and failure-value assumptions.
recommendations.rationale: A single winner erases task specialization and business constraints; a policy turns benchmark evidence into an operational routing decision while preserving uncertainty.
recommendations.uniqueness: This is the moat: private workflow-conditioned, cost-aware, longitudinal model routing rather than a generic public rank or an ephemeral manual verdict.

recommendations.rec: Add frozen validity-plan.json and derived validity-card.json contracts, append validity events to the run JSONL, and require a hash-matched all-pass validity card in the Lane B promoter; any required unknown blocks promotion.
recommendations.rationale: Repeatability and aggregate score do not establish contamination safety, judge validity, adequate power, or gold correctness; a separate gate prevents these threats from being averaged into a plausible score.
recommendations.uniqueness: This turns the mode into a self-auditing private measurement governor whose decisions remain traceable to local tasks, exposure history, judges, gold versions, and costs—evidence public leaderboards and manual bake-offs cannot possess.

recommendations.rec: Replace direct gradeD4 dispatch with a blinded judge-audit orchestrator: independent cross-family audit calls, counterbalanced order and style/provenance probes, reducer-computed rubric totals, per-axis monotonicity tests, and adjudication or unknown on material disagreement rather than averaging.
recommendations.rationale: The current dispute helper is unwired and same-family; even when activated it would measure prompt stance while preserving correlated model bias and could hide disagreement in a mean.
recommendations.uniqueness: Local metamorphic probes can target the exact workflow outputs and failure costs being selected, producing a task-specific judge reliability card unavailable from generic judge rankings.

recommendations.rec: Introduce a versioned item-integrity registry combining component-level gold review, visibility and exposure lineage, fresh same-task contamination references, task-family coverage, and a predeclared small-sample decision rule; replay only affected items when labels or references change.
recommendations.rationale: This closes adaptive fixture leakage, train-test contamination, fixed-N false promotion, and silent oracle corruption while keeping the extra dispatch budget concentrated on decision-sensitive evidence.
recommendations.uniqueness: The registry connects private task provenance and longitudinal corrections directly to operational model routing, which neither public leaderboards nor one-off comparisons can reconstruct.

recommendations.rec: Add a selection-certificate reducer over the existing trial ledger. Block by task_instance_id, cluster repeated decodes by task or task family, apply hard quality/safety/tool/latency constraints first, then estimate paired incremental economic utility with confidence intervals, power or MDE, Holm correction, and utility-weight flip ranges. Emit switch, retain, conditional_route, or insufficient_evidence.
recommendations.rationale: This prevents noisy scalar-score differences and arbitrary five-dimension weights from becoming false WINNER decisions while still producing an operationally useful answer.
recommendations.uniqueness: It converts local task evidence into a decision about whether this workflow should change models, which public rankings and manual comparisons cannot support.

recommendations.rec: Create a trajectory-twin benchmark lane: sample de-identified representative workflow traces, freeze each prefix before the candidate action, replay all candidates against identical repository and tool state, and calibrate fidelity by replaying the incumbent against itself. Mark results invalid when tool responses or resulting state transitions exceed a declared fidelity tolerance.
recommendations.rationale: Representative trace replay measures behavior on the actual task distribution and reveals failures that curated fixtures may omit; self-replay distinguishes model differences from simulator error.
recommendations.uniqueness: This is the moat: only an in-workflow mode can perform controlled model substitution over private, temporally current task and tool state while preserving the surrounding process.

recommendations.rec: Introduce a content-addressed BenchmarkCapsule and workload-envelope matrix. Canonically hash fixtures, task recipe, prompt and tool schemas, scorer and judge versions, decoding settings, exact model endpoint/version, price snapshot, and traffic profile; evaluate candidates across observed context-length, concurrency, prefix-reuse, and request-rate treatments, and expire the certificate when a signed component or traffic distribution changes.
recommendations.rationale: This makes results reproducible, prevents stale model aliases or changed judges from inheriting old evidence, and avoids selecting a model that wins isolated quality tests but violates the real service envelope.
recommendations.uniqueness: The capsule binds model quality to the user's exact workflow contract and deployment conditions rather than a public benchmark's generic environment.

recommendations.rec: Implement a randomized paired treatment lattice: no skill, full skill, full skill with distractors, SKILL.md-only, references-ablated, scripts-ablated, and compatibility-boundary variants; repeat each cell at least three times and bootstrap paired deltas over tasks.
recommendations.rationale: This isolates marginal skill value, component contribution, selection interference, and executor-skill interactions while avoiding model capability masquerading as skill quality.
recommendations.uniqueness: It makes the packaged skill bundle itself the manipulated artifact, which an underlying-model benchmark cannot do.

recommendations.rec: Add a causal-path verifier schema recording discovery, invocation, key-point coverage/order, intermediate state milestones, final artifact tests, cost, and controlled security probes; preserve final-state tests as authoritative unless process compliance is explicitly required.
recommendations.rationale: A final pass/fail cannot distinguish bad guidance from ignored guidance or an execution failure, while trajectory-only scoring can unfairly reject valid alternative solutions.
recommendations.uniqueness: The resulting diagnosis explains how a portable skill changes executor behavior, not merely whether an agent completed a task.

recommendations.rec: Emit a versioned Skill Contribution Certificate containing bundle and evaluator hashes, paired lift confidence intervals, invocation rate, component ablations, task-slice compatibility envelope, negative-transfer cases, cost/security deltas, and evidence-based expiry triggers.
recommendations.rationale: A global score cannot support install, disable, upgrade, or conditional fleet-routing decisions when skill value varies by executor, task slice, dependency version, and cost.
recommendations.uniqueness: This is the moat: certification of a portable capability artifact and its substitution value across executors, something model-benchmark and the other deep modes cannot produce.

recommendations.rec: UNIQUENESS-SHARPENING: add a paired off/auto/forced/placebo attribution matrix to live mode. Extend executor-dispatch.cjs with treatmentArm and executorDescriptor; make live-executor.cjs emit activation/resource canary receipts; add attribution-estimator.cjs producing realizedLift, selectionTax, contentEffect, confidence intervals, cost delta, and executor interactions. Persist each cell with skill bundle hash, task/environment hash, seed, receipts, outcome, tokens, latency, and cost.
recommendations.rationale: This holds the model/task fixed while varying the deployable bundle, then holds the bundle fixed while varying runtime and model. It turns approximate skill-on/off scoring into attributable evidence.
recommendations.uniqueness: Model-benchmark cannot identify whether lost value came from skill discovery, loader semantics, guidance quality, or execution; this matrix can.

recommendations.rec: Split the report and verdict contracts: Mode A emits only ROUTER-CONTRACT-* results; Mode B emits SKILL-LIFT-BENEFICIAL|NEUTRAL|HARMFUL|INCONCLUSIVE using a practical-effect margin and paired interval. Require same-model/different-runtime and same-runtime/different-model contrasts before granting a PORTABLE-SKILL certificate; otherwise label the result executor-confounded.
recommendations.rationale: The current generic PASS can normalize over missing live usefulness, conflating deterministic router correctness with causal skill utility.
recommendations.uniqueness: This creates a release certificate for a portable skill artifact rather than another aggregate model score.

recommendations.rec: Add a pre-dispatch gold-integrity gate. Require goldPolicy=scored|negative|structural-only|pending; block a positive scored scenario when both intent/resource gold are empty; exclude pending and structural-only rows from numerators; emit BLOCKED-BY-GOLD when required coverage is incomplete. Store gold provenance and run a mutation-sensitivity check that proves changing gold changes the score.
recommendations.rationale: Treating empty expected sets as 1.0 permits high router scores without testing routing or discovery, while mutation sensitivity detects inert or decorative gold.
recommendations.uniqueness: A trustworthy skill benchmark validates the bundle's behavioral contract; a model benchmark has no equivalent package-resource oracle.

recommendations.rec: Implement skill-effect-certificate.v1 as the canonical output, backed by randomized no-skill, auto-route, and forced-activation arms; bind every claim to bundle, gold, executor, registry, tool, permission, and dependency digests, and emit explicit issued, withheld, and expired JSONL events.
recommendations.rationale: It unifies uplift, activation, cost, risk, compatibility, empty-gold handling, and freshness into one reproducible decision artifact.
recommendations.uniqueness: Makes skill-benchmark the causal assurance layer for portable behavioral dependencies, which model benchmarks and qualitative improvement modes cannot provide.

recommendations.rec: Add a registry-scaling and composition lane that varies library size, hard-negative similarity, and composition depth, then emits discovery frontiers and confidence-weighted cross-skill interference graphs.
recommendations.rationale: Isolated uplift does not predict whether a skill will be found, correctly composed, or remain safe in a realistic skill library.
recommendations.uniqueness: Certifies the deployability of the skill ecosystem rather than the raw capability of the executor.

recommendations.rec: Add adaptive component attribution using fractional-factorial screening, task/executor blocking, and exact confirmatory trials for selected component pairs; publish minimum-effective-bundle and negative-interaction findings.
recommendations.rationale: It identifies which instructions, references, scripts, examples, and permissions create value without paying for the full subset matrix.
recommendations.uniqueness: Attributes improvement to distributable skill resources rather than leaving the gain confounded with the underlying model.

nextAngleSuggestions: Evaluate 2025-2026 deep-research benchmarks specifically for citation entailment, source independence, claim coverage, and report updateability rather than answer accuracy alone.

Study correction and retraction feeds such as Crossref relation metadata and Retraction Watch for automatic claim invalidation after a completed run.

Benchmark claim-level replication strategies: randomized diff ordering, specialist prompts, model diversity, and tool diversity, measuring marginal recall and validator precision per dollar.
Build an immutable-verifier threat model so a generated reproduction cannot overwrite tests, weaken commands, or otherwise manufacture a green proof receipt.
Study longitudinal review datasets that distinguish fixed, rebutted, ignored, superseded, and workflow-rejected findings instead of treating every unaccepted comment as a false positive.

Design and benchmark an evidence-independence taxonomy: same prompt, changed prompt, fresh context, different model family, different tool family, executable test, symbolic proof, and human confirmation.
Mine review histories for dimension-specific miss rates and use them to learn which changed-code features make security, concurrency, compatibility, or test-quality cells mandatory.

Specify suppression invalidation using program slices and dependency fingerprints rather than line overlap alone.
Benchmark the evidence-independence taxonomy on longitudinal review histories: accepted, rebutted, ignored-risk, superseded, workflow-rejected, and later-regressed findings.

Specify suppression invalidation from program slices, symbol identities, dependency manifests, build flags, and verifier-command digests, then measure false carry-forward versus line-overlap invalidation.
Mine changed-code features and historical misses to learn mandatory concurrency, compatibility, security, and test-quality coverage cells and their minimum evidence depth.
Build an online shadow-canary protocol and compare Bayesian change-point detection against fixed thresholds after model, prompt, or tool updates.
Adversarially test reducer-derived verdicts against truncation, duplicate JSON keys, hidden findings, narrative-verdict contradictions, and final-line injection.
Run controlled context-ladder experiments by reasoning-distance cell to learn when local slices, call graphs, tests, or cross-file contracts improve Type2 and Type3 detection.
Benchmark raw-diff versus semantic-residual review on refactor-heavy PRs, measuring token cost, obligation coverage, accepted findings, and missed regressions.
Design causal filtering for runtime witnesses using repeated seeds, environment normalization, changed-slice proximity, and input intervention.
Evaluate semantic-anchor survival across rebases, symbol moves, generated-code changes, and partial fix commits; measure false finding closure and duplicate resurrection.
Learn a language- and risk-specific verifier escalation policy from historical obligation outcomes, including verifier setup failures and time-to-counterexample.
Benchmark evidence-partition strategies for council seats: BM25 round-robin, semantic communities, contradiction clusters, causal graphs, and adversarially withheld evidence.
Measure seat marginal value with leave-one-seat-out and Shapley-style verdict influence, separating useful independence from theatrical role diversity.
Design a pluralist aggregation contract that distinguishes factual uncertainty, objective trade-offs, constitutional vetoes, and irreducible value disagreement.
Test anti-persuasion controls: anonymous claims, authority-label randomization, confidence masking, order swaps, and minority-evidence survival across rounds.

Build a task-conditioned independence calibration set for architecture planning, refactors, research synthesis, and governance decisions; compare error-correlation N_eff against semantic-diversity proxies.
Design the anonymous claim-graph schema and test verdict invariance under seat-label permutation, order reversal, confidence masking, and provider-name reveal.
Benchmark independence batches against equal-token single-strong-model, self-consistency, majority-vote, and verifier-guided best-of-N baselines; require council-specific marginal value.
Train a packet-local minority sentinel from stance changes, novel-evidence introductions, rebuttal survival, verifier outcomes, and leave-one-seat-out verdict changes.
Build a correlation-aware Dawid-Skene simulation comparing naive conditional independence, provider-cluster tempering, and hierarchical random effects under cloned-seat failure modes.
Design an active comparison-graph policy that selects the next candidate pair by probability-of-best entropy reduction while retaining explicit ties and Condorcet cycles.
Benchmark one-round evidence debate against cross-examination, simultaneous blinded briefs, consultancy, and direct judging stratified by measured information asymmetry.
Define a plural verdict artifact that separates posterior factual beliefs, Pareto-dominated options, constitutional vetoes, minority evidence, and unresolved value orderings without scalarizing them.

Fit a Friedkin-Johnsen influence matrix across benchmark batches—not within one three-seat run—to estimate seat stubbornness, dominant-speaker influence, and provider-cluster dependence, then compare it with the cheaper unsupported-flip gauges.

Design a packet-local counterfactual probe benchmark that measures verdict sensitivity separately to seat identity, response order, argument length, claimed expertise, majority count, and trusted-history poisoning.
Build a cost-normalized admission benchmark comparing single-model, self-consistency, and council runs across routine, hidden-profile, late-evidence, factual-uncertainty, and value-conflict strata.

Specify and adversarially test the plural-verdict schema on fixtures containing Condorcet cycles, factual disagreement, constitutional vetoes, Pareto-dominated options, and irreducible value orderings.
Implement the packet-local counterfactual probe matrix and determine which perturbation metrics best predict real downstream plan changes rather than harmless wording variation.
Test factual-seat calibration using domain seed questions while proving that performance weights cannot suppress normative minority positions.

Design the exact CandidateMatrixArchive schema and compare case-wise Pareto, MAP-Elites, and scalar ranking on synthetic candidate populations with specialists, generalists, regressions, and deceptive high-score candidates.

Build a candidate-blind promotion benchmark measuring whether commit-before-view, hidden executable anchors, cross-family judges, and judge ensembles predict downstream correctness under iterative optimization.

Specify the mutable-genome boundary for deep-improvement: prompts, skills, demonstrations, memory policy, tool descriptions, workflow graph, control code, and mutation policy, with evaluator reachability tests proving each protected asset is inaccessible.
Evaluate profile-scoped promotion and harness-tree routing against one global champion under stable, heterogeneous, and drifting packet workloads.

Build an evaluator-oracle leakage benchmark: vary exact-score visibility, dimension-level feedback, pass/fail-only receipts, query budgets, and cross-candidate memory; measure how quickly a BITE-like optimizer learns promotion exploits.

Specify and attack-test the EvaluatorCapsule capability boundary: hidden fixture storage, judge credentials, grader prompts, cache contents, logs, environment variables, side channels, and signed receipt verification.
Measure whether offline held-out, shadow replay, and limited live canary deltas predict post-ship correctness, cost, and rollback rates across heterogeneous agent workloads.
Implement an EvaluatorCapsule attack harness covering grader prompt leakage, environment variables, cache keys, fixture filenames, timing channels, logs, and forged promotion receipts.
Benchmark isotonic versus logistic judge calibration and paired-bootstrap promotion bounds under sparse gold anchors, heterogeneous profiles, and evaluator-version drift.
Measure how well offline held-out, read-only shadow, and limited canary deltas predict post-ship correctness, cost tails, rollback frequency, and metamorphic violations.

Build the evaluator-oracle leakage benchmark already flagged: vary exact-score, dimension-band, pass/fail, Ladder-thresholded, and terminal-only feedback while measuring canary reconstruction rate per evaluator query.
Attack-test EvaluatorReceiptV2 with replayed receipts, candidate-hash substitution, stale canary epochs, forged signatures, altered gold calibration, and accept-to-ship evaluator drift.
Calibrate regression-envelope false veto rates across sparse agent fixtures: paired exact tests versus bootstrap bounds, weak-segment minimum sizes, and MMD permutation tests on action traces.

Build a profile-substitution benchmark containing adversarial candidates that delete, rename, or weaken self-declared rules; compare current self-derived scoring against one frozen canonical capsule and measure false promotions.
Specify a metamorphic-relation DSL for agent prompts and tool traces, then audit relation validity and shrink quality across routing, coding, review, and safety targets.
Benchmark TextReg-style recurrence and scope penalties against held-out-only selection, measuring cross-domain correctness, prompt growth, narrow-rule accumulation, and rejected useful special cases.
